[Browser Support](#25-browser-support)\n\n### Appendices\n- [Appendix A: Decision Log](#appendix-a-decision-log)\n- [Appendix B: Glossary](#appendix-b-glossary)\n\n---\n\n# PART I: DOMAIN MODEL\n\n## 1. Railway Maintenance Domain\n\n### 1.1 ECM Framework (EU 2019/779)\n\nThe **Entity in Charge of Maintenance (ECM)** framework establishes legal responsibilities for railway vehicle maintenance. While Dubai Metro is outside EU jurisdiction, the ECM framework represents industry best practice.\n\n#### ECM Function Mapping\n\n| Function | Responsibility | MMS V1.0 Feature | Status |\n|----------|----------------|------------------|--------|\n| **ECM-F1** | Maintenance Management | Dashboard KPIs, compliance tracking | Visualization only |\n| **ECM-F2** | Maintenance Development | Interval definitions, plan visualization | Mock data display |\n| **ECM-F3** | Fleet Maintenance Management | Real-time fleet status, conflict detection | Conflict indicators |\n| **ECM-F4** | Maintenance Delivery | Work order visualization, depot allocation | Visual representation |\n\n#### ECM Compliance Requirements\n\n| Requirement | ECM Article | MMS V1.0 Implementation |\n|-------------|-------------|------------------------|\n| Risk assessment | Art. 5.2 | Conflict detection, overdue warnings |\n| Competence management | Art. 5.3 | Not implemented |\n| Documentation | Art. 5.4 | Not implemented |\n| Performance monitoring | Art. 5.5 | KPI display |\n\n---\n\n### 1.2 RAMS Compliance (EN 50126-1)\n\n**RAMS** = Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety\n\nEN 50126-1:2017 defines the railway RAMS lifecycle. MMS visualizes RAMS metrics.\n\n#### RAMS Metric Definitions\n\n| Aspect | Definition | MMS V1.0 Feature |\n|--------|------------|------------------|\n| **Reliability** | Probability of intended function under stated conditions | MTBF display |\n| **Availability** | Ability to perform required function at given instant | Fleet availability % |\n| **Maintainability** | Probability of maintenance within stated time | MTTR display |\n| **Safety** | Freedom from unacceptable risk | Critical task priority |\n\n#### RAMS Targets\n\n| Metric | Formula | Target | Critical Threshold |\n|--------|---------|--------|-------------------|\n| Fleet Availability | (Fit Trains / Total Trains) x 100 | >= 97% | < 95% |\n| MTBF | Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures | Operator-defined | Operator-defined |\n| MTTR | Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs | <= 24 hours | > 48 hours |\n| Schedule Adherence | (On-Time / Scheduled) x 100 | >= 95% | < 90% |\n\n---\n\n### 1.3 EN 15380 Railway Terminology\n\nAll user-facing labels MUST use EN 15380 compliant terminology.\n\n#### Mandatory Term Usage\n\n| EN 15380 Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |\n|---------------|------------|----------------|\n| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |\n| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model | Group, Class, Collection |\n| Depot | Designated maintenance facility | Garage, Workshop, Station |\n| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |\n| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |\n| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |\n| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet | Usage balancing |\n\n#### Maintenance Terminology\n\n| Term | Definition | Context |\n|------|------------|---------|\n| PM (Preventive Maintenance) | Scheduled maintenance at defined intervals | Time or mileage-based |\n| CM (Corrective Maintenance) | Unscheduled repair after failure detection | Reactive |\n| CBM (Condition-Based Maintenance) | Triggered by condition monitoring | Sensor-based |\n| Overhaul | Major refurbishment restoring to original condition | Resets counters |\n| Heavy Maintenance | Significant maintenance requiring extended depot stay | Multi-day duration |\n\n---\n\n## 2. Dubai Metro Context\n\n### 2.1 Fleet Composition\n\n| Fleet | Prefix | Count | Line | Depot | Daily Avg km |\n|-------|--------|-------|------|-------|--------------|\n| Alstom | ALT-001 to ALT-079 | 79 | Red Line | Al Rashidiya | 420-475 km/day |\n| Kinki Sharyo | KNK-001 to KNK-049 | 49 | Green Line | Jebel Ali | 400-454 km/day |\n| **Total** | | **128** | | | |\n\n#### Alstom Fleet Characteristics\n\n| Property | Value |\n|----------|-------|\n| Quantity | 79 trains |\n| Train Designation | ALT-001 through ALT-079 |\n| Cars per Train | 5 |\n| Base Mileage Range | 450,000 - 800,000 km |\n| Assigned Depot | Al Rashidiya |\n\n#### Kinki Sharyo Fleet Characteristics\n\n| Property | Value |\n|----------|-------|\n| Quantity | 49 trains (newer fleet) |\n| Train Designation | KNK-001 through KNK-049 |\n| Cars per Train | 5 |\n| Base Mileage Range | 320,000 - 600,000 km |\n| Assigned Depot | Jebel Ali |\n\n---\n\n### 2.2 Depot Configuration\n\n| Depot ID | Name | Tracks | Weekly Man-Hours | Serves |\n|----------|------|--------|------------------|--------|\n| depot-red | Al Rashidiya | 5 | 2,800 | Alstom fleet |\n| depot-green | Jebel Ali | 4 | 2,400 | Kinki Sharyo fleet |\n\n#### Combined Resources\n\n| Resource | Value | Calculation |\n|----------|-------|-------------|\n| Total Maintenance Tracks | 9 | 5 + 4 |\n| Weekly Man-Hours | 5,200 | 2,800 + 2,400 |\n| Monthly Man-Hours | 20,800 | 5,200 x 4 weeks |\n| Track Capacity (per day) | 2 trains per track | Industry standard |\n| Daily Maintenance Capacity | 18 trains | 9 tracks x 2 trains |\n\n---\n\n### 2.3 Contract Scope\n\n| Parameter | Value |\n|-----------|-------|\n| Contract Duration | 12 years |\n| Operator | Keolis MHI |\n| Client | RTA Dubai |\n| Total Rolling Stock | 128 trains |\n| Planning Horizon | 1-12 years |\n| Maintenance Levels | 4 (A/B/C/D checks) |\n\n---\n\n## 3. Data Model\n\n### 3.1 Train Entity\n\n```typescript\n/**\n * Train entity - represents a single rolling stock unit.\n * JSON-serializable for persistence and API transport.\n */\nexport type AssetType = 'ALSTOM' | 'KINKI';\n\nexport interface Train {\n  /** Unique identifier: \"TRAIN-001\" or \"ALT-001\" */\n  id: string;\n\n  /** Human-readable display name */\n  name: string;\n\n  /**\n   * Fleet type for grouping and maintenance profile\n   * ALSTOM: Red Line fleet (79 trains)\n   * KINKI: Green Line fleet (49 trains)\n   */\n  assetType: AssetType;\n}\n\n/** Extended Train with optional fields */\nexport interface TrainExtended extends Train {\n  carsCount?: number;\n  currentMileageKm?: number;\n  avgDailyMileageKm?: number;\n  status?: 'operational' | 'maintenance' | 'out-of-service';\n  depotId?: string;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 3.2 Task Entity\n\n```typescript\n/**\n * Task types for visual categorization.\n * Maps to color: maintenance=blue, overhaul=orange, inspection=gray\n */\nexport type TaskType = 'maintenance' | 'overhaul' | 'inspection';\n\n/**\n * Maintenance level classification (A/B/C/D check system).\n */\nexport type MaintenanceLevel = 'A' | 'B' | 'C' | 'D';\n\n/**\n * Task entity - represents a scheduled maintenance activity.\n * Dates stored as ISO 8601 strings, NOT Date objects.\n */\nexport interface Task {\n  /** Unique identifier: \"{trainId}-{year}-{index}\" */\n  id: string;\n\n  /** Foreign key reference to Train.id */\n  trainId: string;\n\n  /** Task start date in ISO 8601: \"YYYY-MM-DD\" */\n  startDate: string;\n\n  /** Task end date in ISO 8601: \"YYYY-MM-DD\" */\n  endDate: string;\n\n  /**\n   * Visual category for color coding:\n   * - maintenance: Blue (#1d4ed8)\n   * - overhaul: Orange (#f59e0b)\n   * - inspection: Gray (#6b7280)\n   */\n  type: TaskType;\n\n  /**\n   * Conflict indicator flag.\n   * True if task overlaps another or violates constraints.\n   */\n  hasConflict: boolean;\n\n  /** Optional: ID of task that must complete before this one */\n  predecessorId?: string;\n}\n\n/** Extended Task with optional fields */\nexport interface TaskExtended extends Task {\n  maintenanceLevel?: MaintenanceLevel;\n  manHours?: number;\n  estimatedCost?: number;\n  dependencies?: string[];\n  status?: 'scheduled' | 'in-progress' | 'completed' | 'cancelled';\n  depotId?: string;\n  tracksRequired?: number;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 3.3 TaskRender (Computed)\n\n```typescript\n/**\n * Color mapping for task types.\n */\nexport type TaskColor = 'blue' | 'orange' | 'gray';\n\nexport const TYPE_TO_COLOR: Record<TaskType, TaskColor> = {\n  maintenance: 'blue',\n  overhaul: 'orange',\n  inspection: 'gray',\n};\n\n/**\n * TaskRender - computed values for canvas rendering.\n * NEVER persisted - recomputed on data load.\n */\nexport interface TaskRender {\n  id: string;\n  trainIndex: number;     // Y position (0-based index)\n  startDay: number;       // X position (days since start)\n  duration: number;       // Width in days\n  color: TaskColor;       // Derived from type\n  hasConflict: boolean;\n  type: TaskType;\n  predecessorId?: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Transform persistent Task to render-ready TaskRender.\n */\nfunction transformToRender(\n  task: Task,\n  trainIndexMap: Map<string, number>,\n  datasetStartDate: string\n): TaskRender {\n  const trainIndex = trainIndexMap.get(task.trainId);\n  if (trainIndex === undefined) {\n    throw new Error(`Train not found: ${task.trainId}`);\n  }\n\n  const startDay = daysBetween(datasetStartDate, task.startDate);\n  const duration = daysBetween(task.startDate, task.endDate);\n\n  return {\n    id: task.id,\n    trainIndex,\n    startDay,\n    duration,\n    color: TYPE_TO_COLOR[task.type],\n    hasConflict: task.hasConflict,\n    type: task.type,\n    predecessorId: task.predecessorId,\n  };\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 3.4 Conflict Representation\n\n#### Conflict Detection Formula\n\n```\nOverlap Conflict:\n  (A.startDay <= B.startDay + B.duration) AND\n  (A.startDay + A.duration >= B.startDay) AND\n  (A.trainId == B.trainId)\n```\n\n#### Conflict Visualization\n\n| LOD Level | Conflict Indicator |\n|-----------|-------------------|\n| MACRO | Red intensity in heatmap cells |\n| MESO | Red background on task block |\n| MICRO | Red border + warning icon on task card |\n\nColor specification: Intent.DANGER red (`#cd4246`)\n\n---\n\n### 3.5 View Configuration\n\n```typescript\n/**\n * Dataset metadata for world bounds calculation.\n */\nexport interface DatasetMeta {\n  startDate: string;    // ISO 8601: \"2024-01-01\"\n  trainCount: number;   // Number of trains\n  years: number;        // Time range in years\n}\n\nexport const DEFAULT_DATASET_META: DatasetMeta = {\n  startDate: '2024-01-01',\n  trainCount: 50,\n  years: 10,\n};\n\n/**\n * Canvas view configuration constants.\n */\nexport interface ViewConfig {\n  pxPerDay: number;        // Pixels per day (X scale)\n  pxPerTrain: number;      // Pixels per train row (Y scale)\n  minZoom: number;         // Minimum zoom level\n  maxZoom: number;         // Maximum zoom level\n  zoomSensitivity: number; // Scroll sensitivity\n  lodThresholds: {\n    macro: number;         // Below this = MACRO\n    micro: number;         // Above this = MICRO\n  };\n}\n\nexport const DEFAULT_VIEW_CONFIG: ViewConfig = {\n  pxPerDay: 10,\n  pxPerTrain: 24,\n  minZoom: 0.1,\n  maxZoom: 5.0,\n  zoomSensitivity: 0.002,\n  lodThresholds: {\n    macro: 0.5,\n    micro: 2.0,\n  },\n};\n\n/** Level of Detail enumeration */\nexport type LODLevel = 'MACRO' | 'MESO' | 'MICRO';\n\n/** Runtime viewport state */\nexport interface ViewportState {\n  zoom: number;    // 0.1 to 5.0\n  panX: number;    // Camera X (world units)\n  panY: number;    // Camera Y (world units)\n  lod: LODLevel;   // Derived from zoom\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. Mathematical Formulas\n\n### 4.1 Km Variance (Coefficient of Variation)\n\nPrimary metric for fleet mileage balance. Lower variance = better distribution.\n\n```\nKm Variance % = (Standard Deviation / Mean) * 100\n\nWhere:\n  mean = sum(all train km) / n\n  stddev = sqrt(sum((x_i - mean)^2) / n)\n```\n\n```typescript\nfunction calculateKmVariance(kmValues: number[]): number {\n  if (kmValues.length < 2) return 0;\n\n  const mean = kmValues.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / kmValues.length;\n  if (mean === 0) return 0;\n\n  const squaredDiffs = kmValues.map(km => Math.pow(km - mean, 2));\n  const variance = squaredDiffs.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / kmValues.length;\n  const stdDev = Math.sqrt(variance);\n\n  return (stdDev / mean) * 100;\n}\n```\n\n| Status | Variance | Indicator |\n|--------|----------|-----------|\n| Green (Healthy) | <= 10% | Green badge |\n| Yellow (Warning) | 10% - 15% | Yellow badge |\n| Red (Critical) | > 15% | Red badge |\n\n---\n\n### 4.2 Fleet Availability\n\n```\nFleet Availability % = (Fit Trains / Total Trains) * 100\n\nTargets:\n  Target: >= 97%\n  Critical: < 95%\n```\n\n```typescript\nfunction calculateFleetAvailability(fitTrains: number, totalTrains: number): number {\n  if (totalTrains === 0) return 0;\n  return (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4.3 MTBF / MTTR / MDBF\n\n```\nMTBF = Total Operating Hours / Number of Failures (Hours)\nMDBF = Total Operating km / Number of Failures (Kilometers)\nMTTR = Total Downtime Hours / Number of Repairs (Hours)\n       Target: <= 24 hours, Critical: > 48 hours\n```\n\n```typescript\ninterface FailureMetrics {\n  mtbf: number;\n  mdbf: number;\n  mttr: number;\n}\n\nfunction calculateFailureMetrics(\n  operatingHours: number,\n  operatingKm: number,\n  downtimeHours: number,\n  failureCount: number,\n  repairCount: number\n): FailureMetrics {\n  return {\n    mtbf: failureCount > 0 ? operatingHours / failureCount : Infinity,\n    mdbf: failureCount > 0 ? operatingKm / failureCount : Infinity,\n    mttr: repairCount > 0 ? downtimeHours / repairCount : 0,\n  };\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4.4 Coordinate System\n\n#### Constants\n\n| Constant | Value | Description |\n|----------|-------|-------------|\n| `PX_PER_DAY` | 10 pixels | 1 day = 10 pixels on X axis |\n| `PX_PER_TRAIN` | 24 pixels | 1 train row = 24 pixels on Y axis |\n| `minZoom` | 0.1 | Minimum zoom (zoomed out) |\n| `maxZoom` | 5.0 | Maximum zoom (zoomed in) |\n\n#### Coordinate Conversions\n\n```typescript\nconst PX_PER_DAY = 10;\nconst PX_PER_TRAIN = 24;\n\nfunction dayToX(day: number): number {\n  return day * PX_PER_DAY;\n}\n\nfunction trainIndexToY(index: number): number {\n  return -index * PX_PER_TRAIN;\n}\n\nfunction screenToWorld(\n  screenX: number, screenY: number,\n  cameraX: number, cameraY: number,\n  zoom: number,\n  viewportWidth: number, viewportHeight: number\n): { worldX: number; worldY: number } {\n  return {\n    worldX: cameraX + (screenX - viewportWidth / 2) / zoom,\n    worldY: cameraY + (screenY - viewportHeight / 2) / zoom,\n  };\n}\n```\n\n#### LOD Thresholds\n\n| LOD Level | Zoom Range | Time Scale | Rendering |\n|-----------|------------|------------|-----------|\n| MACRO | < 0.5 | 10-20 years | Heatmap (aggregated) |\n| MESO | 0.5 - 2.0 | 2-5 years | Task blocks (instanced) |\n| MICRO | >= 2.0 | 4-8 weeks | Detailed cards (HTML) |\n\n#### Blend Zone\n\n```typescript\nconst BLEND_ZONE_SIZE = 0.15;\n\nfunction calculateOpacity(\n  zoom: number,\n  threshold: number,\n  direction: 'in' | 'out'\n): number {\n  const t = (zoom - threshold) / BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;\n  const clamped = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, t));\n  return direction === 'in' ? clamped : 1 - clamped;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4.5 Km Penalty Calculation (Solver)\n\nUsed by allocation solver to prioritize under-utilized trains.\n\n```\ndeviation_pct = |current_km - target_km| / max(target_km, 1) * 100\n\nIf current_km < target_km:\n  penalty = -deviation_pct * 10  (BONUS)\nIf current_km >= target_km:\n  penalty = +deviation_pct * 10  (PENALTY)\n```\n\n```typescript\nfunction calculateKmPenalty(\n  currentKm: number,\n  targetKm: number,\n  fleetAvgKm: number\n): number {\n  const effectiveTarget = targetKm || fleetAvgKm;\n  if (effectiveTarget === 0) return 0;\n\n  const deviationPct = Math.abs(currentKm - effectiveTarget) / effectiveTarget * 100;\n\n  if (currentKm < effectiveTarget) {\n    return -Math.round(deviationPct * 10);\n  } else {\n    return Math.round(deviationPct * 10);\n  }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4.6 Haversine Distance (Dead Running)\n\nGreat circle distance between two geographic points.\n\n```\na = sin^2(delta_lat/2) + cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin^2(delta_lon/2)\nc = 2 * atan2(sqrt(a), sqrt(1-a))\ndistance = R * c\n\nWhere: R = 6371 km (Earth's radius)\n```\n\n```typescript\nfunction haversineDistance(\n  lat1: number, lon1: number,\n  lat2: number, lon2: number\n): number {\n  const R = 6371;\n  const toRad = (deg: number) => deg * Math.PI / 180;\n\n  const lat1Rad = toRad(lat1);\n  const lat2Rad = toRad(lat2);\n  const deltaLat = toRad(lat2 - lat1);\n  const deltaLon = toRad(lon2 - lon1);\n\n  const a = Math.sin(deltaLat / 2) ** 2 +\n            Math.cos(lat1Rad) * Math.cos(lat2Rad) *\n            Math.sin(deltaLon / 2) ** 2;\n\n  const c = 2 * Math.atan2(Math.sqrt(a), Math.sqrt(1 - a));\n\n  return R * c;\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. Maintenance Level Mapping\n\n### Authoritative Mapping Table\n\n| Code | Railway Term | Display | Interval (km) | Tolerance | Duration | Man-Hours | Tracks | Cost |\n|------|--------------|---------|---------------|-----------|----------|-----------|--------|------|\n| A | PM1 | A-Check | 10,000 | +/- 2,000 | 4h | 8 | 1 | $2,500 |\n| B | PM2 | B-Check | 50,000 | +/- 5,000 | 16h | 32 | 1 | $8,500 |\n| C | PM3 | C-Check | 200,000 | +/- 20,000 | 72h | 160 | 1 | $45,000 |\n| D | HM | D-Overhaul | 800,000 | +/- 50,000 | 480h | 1,200 | 2 | $180,000 |\n\n#### Terminology Key\n\n| Term | Meaning |\n|------|---------|\n| **PM1** | Preventive Maintenance Level 1 (Light) |\n| **PM2** | Preventive Maintenance Level 2 (Intermediate) |\n| **PM3** | Preventive Maintenance Level 3 (Scheduled) |\n| **HM** | Heavy Maintenance / Major Overhaul |\n| **OVH** | Full Overhaul / Refurbishment (resets all counters) |\n\n### Maintenance Cascade\n\n```\nFleet Type (e.g., Alstom Red Line)\n |\n +-- Maintenance Profile\n     |\n     +-- PM1 / A-Check (every 10,000 km)\n     |   Duration: 4 hours\n     |\n     +-- PM2 / B-Check (every 50,000 km)\n     |   Duration: 16 hours, INCLUDES PM1 tasks\n     |\n     +-- PM3 / C-Check (every 200,000 km)\n     |   Duration: 72 hours, INCLUDES PM1 + PM2 tasks\n     |\n     +-- HM / D-Overhaul (every 800,000 km)\n         Duration: 480 hours, RESETS counters\n```\n\n### Maintenance Triggers\n\n| Trigger Type | Description | Best For |\n|--------------|-------------|----------|\n| Time-based | Calendar intervals | Simple assets, regulatory |\n| Mileage-based | Odometer intervals (primary) | Wear-dependent components |\n| Condition-based | Sensor thresholds | Critical components |\n| Hybrid | Combined triggers | Most applications |\n\n---\n\n## 6. Domain Rules\n\n### 6.1 Hard Constraints (Never Violate)\n\n| ID | Rule | Rationale |\n|----|------|-----------|\n| HC-1 | Unfit trains NEVER assigned to service | Safety-critical |\n| HC-2 | Maintenance windows are immutable | Planned downtime |\n| HC-3 | Aptitude requirements are binary | Route compatibility |\n| HC-4 | Depot capacity is physical | Track slots |\n| HC-5 | Service cannot be cancelled for balancing | Operations > Optimization |\n\n### 6.2 Soft Constraints (Optimize Within)\n\n| ID | Rule | Weight |\n|----|------|--------|\n| SC-1 | Km balancing priority | 1000 (HIGH) |\n| SC-2 | Dead running minimization | 100 (MEDIUM) |\n| SC-3 | Maintenance depot proximity | 10 (LOW) |\n\n### 6.3 Tolerance Windows\n\n| Level | Tolerance | Overrun Warning | Critical |\n|-------|-----------|-----------------|----------|\n| PM1 / A-Check | +/- 2,000 km | 95% | 105% |\n| PM2 / B-Check | +/- 5,000 km | 95% | 105% |\n| PM3 / C-Check | +/- 20,000 km | 95% | 105% |\n| HM / D-Overhaul | +/- 50,000 km | 95% | 105% |\n\n---\n\n## 7. Train State Machine\n\n```\n    +-----------------------------------------------+\n    |                                               |\n    v                                               |\n+-------+     +------------+     +------------+     |\n|  FIT  |---->| IN-SERVICE |---->| AVAILABLE  |----+\n+---+---+     +------------+     +-----+------+\n    |                                  |\n    |         +------------+           |\n    +-------->|   UNFIT    |<----------+\n              +-----+------+\n                    |\n                    v\n              +------------+\n              |MAINTENANCE |\n              +-----+------+\n                    |\n                    v\n              +------------+\n              |    FIT     |\n              +------------+\n```\n\n| State | Definition | Transitions |\n|-------|------------|-------------|\n| **FIT** | Train operationally ready | IN-SERVICE, UNFIT |\n| **IN-SERVICE** | Running revenue service | AVAILABLE |\n| **AVAILABLE** | Fit but not assigned | IN-SERVICE, UNFIT |\n| **UNFIT** | Requires maintenance | MAINTENANCE |\n| **MAINTENANCE** | Undergoing maintenance | FIT |\n\n---\n\n## 8. Planning Hierarchy\n\n```\n+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n|                MASTER PLAN (Annual/Multi-Year)               |\n|  - Resource allocation across contract period                |\n|  - Major overhaul scheduling (HM/D-Check)                    |\n+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n|                     MONTHLY PLAN                             |\n|  - Medium-scale repairs allocation (PM2/PM3)                 |\n|  - Workload balancing across weeks                           |\n+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n|                      WEEKLY PLAN                             |\n|  - Detailed work orders                                      |\n|  - Staff rostering and track allocation                      |\n+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n|                      DAILY PLAN                              |\n|  - Execution scheduling (PM1/A-Check)                        |\n|  - Real-time adjustments                                     |\n+-------------------------------------------------------------+\n```\n\n### Time Zone Concept (Frozen Periods)\n\n| Horizon | Zone | Constraint | Allowed Changes |\n|---------|------|------------|-----------------|\n| T > 12 weeks | **Liquid** | Soft | Structure, Volume, Frequency |\n| T-12 to T-7 | **Slushy** | Medium | Timing, Resource Allocation |\n| T-7 to T-48h | **Frozen** | Hard | Emergency Only |\n| T < 48h | **Execution** | Fixed | Break-fix only |\n\n### MMS LOD Mapping\n\n| Planning Level | MMS LOD | Zoom Range |\n|----------------|---------|------------|\n| Master Plan | MACRO | < 0.5x |\n| Monthly Plan | MESO | 0.5x - 1.0x |\n| Weekly Plan | MESO | 1.0x - 2.0x |\n| Daily Plan | MICRO | > 2.0x |\n\n---\n\n# PART II: TECHNICAL ARCHITECTURE\n\n## 9. System Overview\n\n### 9.1 Architecture Diagram\n\n```\n+==============================================================================+\n|                          MMS V1.0 GENESIS ARCHITECTURE                        |\n+==============================================================================+\n\n+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n|                              REACT APPLICATION                                |\n|                         (Vite 5.x + React 18.3.1)                            |\n+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n|                                                                               |\n|  +------------------+  +---------------------+  +------------------------+   |\n|  |    App.tsx       |  |   canvasStore       |  |   RBushSpatialIndex    |   |\n|  |    (Layout)      |<-|   (@zazcart/tools)  |<-|   (O(log n) queries)   |   |\n|  +------------------+  +---------------------+  +------------------------+   |\n|           |                      |                         |                  |\n+-----------|----------------------|-------------------------|------------------+\n            v                      v                         v\n+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n|                         LOD DECISION LAYER                                    |\n|    +----------------+    +----------------+    +----------------+             |\n|    |  zoom < 0.5    |    | 0.5 <= z < 2.0 |    |   zoom >= 2.0  |             |\n|    |     MACRO      |    |      MESO      |    |     MICRO      |             |\n|    +-------+--------+    +-------+--------+    +-------+--------+             |\n+------------|---------------------|---------------------|----------------------+\n             v                     v                     v\n+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n|                          RENDERING LAYERS                                     |\n|  +------------------+  +---------------------+  +------------------------+   |\n|  |   MacroLayer     |  |    MesoLayer        |  |    MicroLayer          |   |\n|  | InstancedMesh    |  |  InstancedMesh      |  |  InstancedMesh         |   |\n|  | (heatmap cells)  |  |  (task blocks)      |  |  + HTML labels         |   |\n|  | Cells: 600       |  |  Pre-alloc: 100k    |  |  + Line2 curves        |   |\n|  +------------------+  +---------------------+  +------------------------+   |\n+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n|                 REACT THREE FIBER 8.x + THREE.JS 0.170.x                     |\n|                         OrthographicCamera                                    |\n|                    X = Time (days) | Y = Train Index                         |\n+------------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n```\n\n### 9.2 Technology Stack\n\n| Layer | Technology | Version | Purpose |\n|-------|------------|---------|---------|\n| **Build Tool** | Vite | 5.x | Fast HMR, ESM-first |\n| **UI Framework** | React | 18.3.1 | Required for BlueprintJS 6 |\n| **UI Components** | BlueprintJS | 6.1.0 | Enterprise UI components |\n| **3D Renderer** | React Three Fiber | 8.17.10 | Declarative Three.js |\n| **3D Helpers** | drei | 9.114.3 | R3F ecosystem |\n| **3D Engine** | Three.js | 0.170.0 | WebGL rendering |\n| **State** | @zazcart/tools | 0.1.0 | Slice-based state machine |\n| **Spatial Index** | RBush | 4.0.1 | R-tree for O(log n) queries |\n| **Immutability** | Immer | 10.2.0 | Reducer state updates |\n\n### 9.3 Magna Rules\n\nThese rules are INVIOLABLE for V1.0:\n\n| Rule | Constraint | Rationale |\n|------|------------|-----------|\n| **MAGNA-001** | React 18.3.1 (NOT 19) | BlueprintJS 6 requires React 18 |\n| **MAGNA-002** | R3F 8.x + drei 9.x | Pairs with React 18 |\n| **MAGNA-003** | 60fps minimum | Demo success criterion |\n| **MAGNA-004** | Desktop-first | Mobile is deferred |\n| **MAGNA-005** | Mock data only | No real mileage integration |\n| **MAGNA-006** | @zazcart/tools for state | Avoid useState stale closures |\n| **MAGNA-007** | RBush for all queries | O(log n) is mandatory |\n\n---\n\n## 10. State Management\n\n### 10.1 Library Overview\n\n**Package:** `@zazcart/tools` (located at `packages/toolkit`)\n\nThe library provides a slice-based state machine pattern with:\n- Immer-powered immutable updates\n- React 18 `useSyncExternalStore` integration\n- Zero-allocation option for 60fps updates\n\n**Note:** `@zazcart/tools` and `@belt/tools` are the SAME library.\n\n### 10.2 createMachine Pattern\n\n```typescript\n// File: apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts\nimport { createMachine } from '@zazcart/tools';\nimport { viewportSlice } from './viewport';\nimport { interactionSlice } from './interaction';\n\nexport const canvasMachine = createMachine({\n  id: 'canvas',\n  slices: {\n    viewport: viewportSlice,\n    interaction: interactionSlice,\n  },\n  context: {\n    userID: null,\n  },\n  // CRITICAL: Disable metadata tracking for 60fps\n  metadata: false,\n});\n\nexport const useCanvas = canvasMachine.useMachine;\nexport const useCanvasSelector = canvasMachine.useSelector;\nexport const canvasActions = canvasMachine.actions;\n\nexport const canvasStore = {\n  getState: canvasMachine.getState,\n  dispatch: canvasMachine.dispatch,\n  subscribe: canvasMachine.subscribe,\n};\n```\n\n### 10.3 Slice Definitions\n\n#### Viewport Slice\n\n```typescript\n// File: apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts\nimport { createSyncSlice } from '@zazcart/tools';\n\nexport interface CameraPosition {\n  x: number;\n  y: number;\n  zoom: number;\n}\n\nexport interface ViewportSliceState {\n  camera: CameraPosition;\n  canvasSize: { width: number; height: number };\n  isInitialized: boolean;\n  momentum: { velocityX: number; velocityY: number; isActive: boolean };\n  animation: {\n    isAnimating: boolean;\n    startTime: number;\n    duration: number;\n    startX: number; startY: number; startZoom: number;\n    targetX: number; targetY: number; targetZoom: number;\n  };\n  isPanning: boolean;\n  panStart: CameraPosition;\n  cameraPosAtPanStart: CameraPosition;\n}\n\nexport const viewportSlice = createSyncSlice({\n  id: 'viewport',\n  initial: {\n    camera: { x: 0, y: 0, zoom: 1 },\n    canvasSize: { width: 0, height: 0 },\n    isInitialized: false,\n    momentum: { velocityX: 0, velocityY: 0, isActive: false },\n    animation: {\n      isAnimating: false, startTime: 0, duration: 250,\n      startX: 0, startY: 0, startZoom: 1,\n      targetX: 0, targetY: 0, targetZoom: 1,\n    },\n    isPanning: false,\n    panStart: { x: 0, y: 0, zoom: 1 },\n    cameraPosAtPanStart: { x: 0, y: 0, zoom: 1 },\n  },\n  cache: { policy: 'session' },\n  reducers: {\n    setCamera(state, payload: CameraPosition) { state.camera = payload; },\n    setZoom(state, payload: number) { state.camera.zoom = payload; },\n    setCanvasSize(state, payload: { width: number; height: number }) {\n      state.canvasSize = payload;\n      state.isInitialized = true;\n    },\n    startPan(state, payload: { x: number; y: number }) {\n      state.isPanning = true;\n      state.panStart = { x: payload.x, y: payload.y, zoom: state.camera.zoom };\n      state.cameraPosAtPanStart = { ...state.camera };\n    },\n    endPan(state) { state.isPanning = false; },\n    startMomentum(state, payload: { velocityX: number; velocityY: number }) {\n      state.momentum = { ...payload, isActive: true };\n    },\n    stopMomentum(state) {\n      state.momentum = { velocityX: 0, velocityY: 0, isActive: false };\n    },\n    reset() { return viewportSlice.initial; },\n  },\n});\n```\n\n#### Interaction Slice\n\n```typescript\n// File: apps/canvas/src/state/interaction.ts\nimport { createSyncSlice } from '@zazcart/tools';\n\nexport interface InteractionSliceState {\n  hoveredTaskId: string | null;\n  selectedTaskId: string | null;\n  focusedTaskId: string | null;\n  mousePosition: { x: number; y: number } | null;\n}\n\nexport const interactionSlice = createSyncSlice({\n  id: 'interaction',\n  initial: {\n    hoveredTaskId: null,\n    selectedTaskId: null,\n    focusedTaskId: null,\n    mousePosition: null,\n  },\n  cache: { policy: 'session' },\n  reducers: {\n    setHoveredTask(state, payload: string | null) { state.hoveredTaskId = payload; },\n    setSelectedTask(state, payload: string | null) { state.selectedTaskId = payload; },\n    setFocusedTask(state, payload: string | null) { state.focusedTaskId = payload; },\n    setMousePosition(state, payload: { x: number; y: number } | null) {\n      state.mousePosition = payload;\n    },\n    clearSelection(state) {\n      state.selectedTaskId = null;\n      state.focusedTaskId = null;\n    },\n  },\n});\n```\n\n### 10.4 Usage Patterns\n\n**Pattern 1: React Component with Selector**\n\n```typescript\nimport { useCanvasSelector, canvasActions, canvasStore } from '../state';\n\nfunction ZoomControls() {\n  const zoom = useCanvasSelector(s => s.viewport.camera.zoom);\n\n  const handleZoomIn = () => {\n    canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.setZoom(zoom * 1.2));\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <button onClick={handleZoomIn}>+</button>\n  );\n}\n```\n\n**Pattern 2: Animation Loop (NO React Hooks)**\n\n```typescript\nimport { useFrame } from '@react-three/fiber';\nimport { canvasStore, canvasActions } from '../state';\n\nfunction CameraAnimator() {\n  useFrame(() => {\n    // CORRECT: Get fresh state every frame\n    const state = canvasStore.getState();\n    const { animation, camera } = state.viewport;\n\n    if (!animation.isAnimating) return;\n\n    const elapsed = Date.now() - animation.startTime;\n    const progress = Math.min(elapsed / animation.duration, 1);\n\n    // Update camera...\n  });\n\n  return null;\n}\n```\n\n**Pattern 3: Event Handler**\n\n```typescript\nconst handleWheel = useCallback((event: WheelEvent) => {\n  event.preventDefault();\n  const state = canvasStore.getState();\n  const { camera } = state.viewport;\n\n  const zoomDelta = -event.deltaY * ZOOM_SENSITIVITY;\n  const newZoom = clamp(camera.zoom * (1 + zoomDelta), MIN_ZOOM, MAX_ZOOM);\n\n  canvasStore.dispatch(canvasActions.viewport.setCamera({\n    x: camera.x, y: camera.y, zoom: newZoom,\n  }));\n}, []);\n```\n\n---\n\n## 11. Canvas Architecture\n\n### 11.1 React Three Fiber Integration\n\n**Version Pairing (Critical):**\n\n| React Version | R3F Version | drei Version |\n|---------------|-------------|--------------|\n| **React 18** | **R3F 8.x** | **drei 9.x** |\n| React 19 | R3F 9.x | drei 10.x |\n\n```typescript\n// Camera configuration\nconst CAMERA_NEAR = -1000;\nconst CAMERA_FAR = 1000;\nconst INITIAL_ZOOM = 1;\n\n<Canvas\n  orthographic\n  camera={{\n    position: [0, 0, 100],\n    zoom: INITIAL_ZOOM,\n    near: CAMERA_NEAR,\n    far: CAMERA_FAR,\n  }}\n  gl={{\n    antialias: true,\n    alpha: false,\n    powerPreference: 'high-performance',\n  }}\n>\n  <CameraController />\n  <LODLayers />\n</Canvas>\n```\n\n### 11.2 Coordinate System\n\n```\n                                    +X (Time - Days)\n                                    |\n        (-) Past                    |                    (+) Future\n   <--------------------------------+--------------------------------->\n                                    |\n                   Day 0 = meta.startDate (2024-01-01)\n\n+Y (Trains - negative because canvas Y grows down)\n|\n|  Train 0:  y = 0\n|  Train 1:  y = -24 (PX_PER_TRAIN)\n|  Train 2:  y = -48\n|  ...\n|  Train 49: y = -1176\nv\n\nConstants:\n  PX_PER_DAY = 10\n  PX_PER_TRAIN = 24\n\nViewport:\n  Camera position (x, y) is CENTER of visible area\n  Visible width = canvasWidth / zoom\n  Visible height = canvasHeight / zoom\n```\n\n---\n\n## 12. LOD System\n\n### 12.1 Three-Layer Architecture\n\n| Layer | Zoom Range | Visualization | Max Instances |\n|-------|------------|---------------|---------------|\n| **MACRO** | < 0.5x | Heatmap cells | 600 |\n| **MESO** | 0.5x - 2.0x | Task blocks | 100,000 |\n| **MICRO** | > 2.0x | HTML labels + curves | 50 labels |\n\n### 12.2 Crossfade Transitions\n\n```typescript\nconst LOD_THRESHOLDS = {\n  macroToMeso: 0.5,\n  mesoToMicro: 2.0,\n};\nconst BLEND_ZONE_SIZE = 0.15;\n\nfunction calculateLayerOpacities(zoom: number): {\n  macro: number; meso: number; micro: number;\n} {\n  const { macroToMeso, mesoToMicro } = LOD_THRESHOLDS;\n\n  const macroEnd = macroToMeso - BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;\n  const mesoStart = macroToMeso + BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;\n  const mesoEnd = mesoToMicro - BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;\n  const microStart = mesoToMicro + BLEND_ZONE_SIZE;\n\n  let macro = 0, meso = 0, micro = 0;\n\n  if (zoom <= macroEnd) macro = 1;\n  else if (zoom < mesoStart) {\n    const t = (zoom - macroEnd) / (mesoStart - macroEnd);\n    macro = 1 - t;\n    meso = t;\n  } else if (zoom <= mesoEnd) {\n    meso = 1;\n  } else if (zoom < microStart) {\n    const t = (zoom - mesoEnd) / (microStart - mesoEnd);\n    meso = 1 - t;\n    micro = t;\n  } else {\n    micro = 1;\n  }\n\n  return { macro, meso, micro };\n}\n```\n\n**Material Configuration:**\n\n```typescript\n<meshBasicMaterial\n  toneMapped={false}\n  transparent={opacity < 1}\n  opacity={opacity}\n  depthWrite={opacity >= 0.5}  // Prevent z-fighting\n/>\n```\n\n### 12.3 Layer Implementations\n\n#### MacroLayer\n\n```typescript\n// InstancedMesh with heatmap coloring\nconst CELL_WIDTH = 30 * 10;  // ~30 days * pxPerDay\nconst CELL_HEIGHT = 24;\n\nexport function MacroLayer({ tasks, trainCount, opacity = 1 }) {\n  const meshRef = useRef<InstancedMesh>(null);\n  const tempObject = useMemo(() => new Object3D(), []);\n  const tempColor = useMemo(() => new Color(), []);\n\n  const totalMonths = 10 * 12;\n  const totalCells = trainCount * totalMonths;\n\n  const densityGrid = useMemo(\n    () => calculateDensityGrid(tasks, trainCount, totalMonths),\n    [tasks, trainCount, totalMonths]\n  );\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!meshRef.current) return;\n    // Update instance matrices and colors based on density...\n  }, [densityGrid]);\n\n  return (\n    <instancedMesh ref={meshRef} args={[undefined, undefined, totalCells]}>\n      <planeGeometry args={[1, 1]} />\n      <meshBasicMaterial toneMapped={false} transparent={opacity < 1}\n        opacity={opacity} depthWrite={opacity >= 0.5} vertexColors />\n    </instancedMesh>\n  );\n}\n```\n\n#### MesoLayer\n\n```typescript\nconst MAX_INSTANCES = 100000;\nconst TASK_HEIGHT = 18;\n\nconst COLORS = {\n  blue: '#3b82f6',\n  orange: '#f59e0b',\n  gray: '#9ca3af',\n};\nconst CONFLICT_COLOR = '#ef4444';\n\nexport function MesoLayer({ tasks, opacity = 1 }) {\n  const meshRef = useRef<InstancedMesh>(null);\n  const tempObject = useMemo(() => new Object3D(), []);\n  const tempColor = useMemo(() => new Color(), []);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!meshRef.current) return;\n    meshRef.current.count = tasks.length;\n\n    tasks.forEach((task, i) => {\n      const width = task.duration * PX_PER_DAY;\n      const x = task.startDay * PX_PER_DAY + width / 2;\n      const y = -task.trainIndex * PX_PER_TRAIN - PX_PER_TRAIN / 2;\n\n      tempObject.position.set(x, y, 0);\n      tempObject.scale.set(width, TASK_HEIGHT, 1);\n      tempObject.updateMatrix();\n      meshRef.current.setMatrixAt(i, tempObject.matrix);\n\n      const color = task.hasConflict ? CONFLICT_COLOR : COLORS[task.color];\n      tempColor.set(color);\n      meshRef.current.setColorAt(i, tempColor);\n    });\n\n    meshRef.current.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate = true;\n    if (meshRef.current.instanceColor) {\n      meshRef.current.instanceColor.needsUpdate = true;\n    }\n  }, [tasks]);\n\n  return (\n    <instancedMesh ref={meshRef} args={[undefined, undefined, MAX_INSTANCES]}>\n      <planeGeometry args={[1, 1]} />\n      <meshBasicMaterial toneMapped={false} transparent={opacity < 1}\n        opacity={opacity} depthWrite={opacity >= 0.5} vertexColors />\n    </instancedMesh>\n  );\n}\n```\n\n#### MicroLayer\n\n```typescript\nconst MAX_VISIBLE_LABELS = 50;\n\nexport function MicroLayer({ tasks, viewportBounds, opacity = 1 }) {\n  const visibleTasks = useMemo(() => {\n    if (!viewportBounds) return tasks.slice(0, MAX_VISIBLE_LABELS);\n    const visible = spatialIndex.queryViewport(viewportBounds);\n    return visible.slice(0, MAX_VISIBLE_LABELS);\n  }, [tasks, viewportBounds]);\n\n  return (\n    <group>\n      <TaskBlocks tasks={tasks} opacity={opacity} />\n      {visibleTasks.map(task => (\n        <TaskLabel key={task.id} task={task} opacity={opacity} />\n      ))}\n      <DependencyLines tasks={visibleTasks} opacity={opacity} />\n    </group>\n  );\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 13. Performance Architecture\n\n### 13.1 Performance Requirements\n\n| Metric | Target | Confidence |\n|--------|--------|------------|\n| **Frame Rate** | 60fps constant | 70% (desktop) |\n| **Frame Budget** | < 16.67ms | 70% (desktop) |\n| **Viewport Query** | < 1ms | 95% (verified) |\n| **LOD Transition** | < 100ms | 90% |\n| **Memory** | < 100MB stable | 80% |\n\n### 13.2 InstancedMesh Pre-allocation\n\n**Problem:** Dynamic count causes GPU buffer reallocation.\n\n```typescript\n// WRONG: Recreates buffer when length changes\n<instancedMesh args={[geometry, material, tasks.length]}>\n\n// CORRECT: Pre-allocate, control with mesh.count\nconst MAX_INSTANCES = 100000;\n<instancedMesh ref={meshRef} args={[geometry, material, MAX_INSTANCES]}>\n\n// In useEffect:\nmesh.count = actualTaskCount;\n```\n\n**Memory Cost:** 100k instances x 64 bytes = ~6.4MB GPU memory\n\n### 13.3 RBush Spatial Indexing\n\n**MANDATORY: All viewport queries must use RBush. Array.filter() is FORBIDDEN.**\n\n```typescript\nimport RBush from 'rbush';\n\ninterface RBushItem {\n  minX: number; minY: number;\n  maxX: number; maxY: number;\n  id: string;\n}\n\nexport class RBushSpatialIndex {\n  private tree: RBush<RBushItem>;\n  private itemsById: Map<string, RBushItem> = new Map();\n\n  constructor() { this.tree = new RBush(); }\n\n  load(tasks: TaskRender[]): void {\n    this.tree.clear();\n    this.itemsById.clear();\n\n    const items: RBushItem[] = tasks.map(task => ({\n      minX: task.startDay * PX_PER_DAY,\n      maxX: (task.startDay + task.duration) * PX_PER_DAY,\n      minY: -task.trainIndex * PX_PER_TRAIN - PX_PER_TRAIN,\n      maxY: -task.trainIndex * PX_PER_TRAIN,\n      id: task.id,\n    }));\n\n    for (const item of items) {\n      this.itemsById.set(item.id, item);\n    }\n\n    this.tree.load(items);  // Bulk load O(n log n)\n  }\n\n  queryViewport(bounds: ViewportBounds): string[] {\n    const OVERSCAN = 100;\n    const results = this.tree.search({\n      minX: bounds.minX - OVERSCAN,\n      maxX: bounds.maxX + OVERSCAN,\n      minY: bounds.minY - OVERSCAN,\n      maxY: bounds.maxY + OVERSCAN,\n    });\n    return results.map(item => item.id);\n  }\n\n  queryNearest(x: number, y: number, maxDistance = 50): { id: string; distance: number } | null {\n    let radius = 20;\n    let nearest: { id: string; distance: number } | null = null;\n\n    while (radius <= maxDistance) {\n      const candidates = this.tree.search({\n        minX: x - radius, maxX: x + radius,\n        minY: y - radius, maxY: y + radius,\n      });\n\n      for (const item of candidates) {\n        const centerX = (item.minX + item.maxX) / 2;\n        const centerY = (item.minY + item.maxY) / 2;\n        const dist = Math.sqrt((x - centerX) ** 2 + (y - centerY) ** 2);\n\n        if (dist <= maxDistance && (!nearest || dist < nearest.distance)) {\n          nearest = { id: item.id, distance: dist };\n        }\n      }\n\n      if (nearest) return nearest;\n      radius *= 2;\n    }\n\n    return nearest;\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 13.4 Object Pooling\n\n**MANDATORY: GC is the primary bottleneck in WebGL applications.**\n\n```typescript\n// In component scope (created once, reused every frame)\nconst tempObject = useMemo(() => new Object3D(), []);\nconst tempColor = useMemo(() => new Color(), []);\nconst tempVector = useMemo(() => new Vector3(), []);\n\n// In animation loop\nuseFrame(() => {\n  // CORRECT: Reuse pooled objects\n  tempVector.set(x, y, z);\n  ref.current.position.copy(tempVector);\n\n  // WRONG: Creates new object every frame\n  // ref.current.position.copy(new Vector3(x, y, z));\n});\n```\n\n### 13.5 GC Avoidance\n\n| Strategy | Implementation | Impact |\n|----------|----------------|--------|\n| Object Pooling | useMemo for temp objects | High |\n| metadata: false | State machine config | Medium |\n| TypedArrays | EntityStore SoA pattern | High |\n| Pre-allocation | InstancedMesh MAX_INSTANCES | High |\n| Bulk operations | RBush.load() | Medium |\n\n**Frame Budget: 16.67ms**\n\n| Operation | Budget | Priority |\n|-----------|--------|----------|\n| GPU Render | ~8ms | Critical |\n| JavaScript | ~5ms | Critical |\n| Layout/Paint | ~2ms | Low |\n| GC | **0ms target** | **Eliminate** |\n\n---\n\n## 14. Data Flow\n\n### Data Generation Pipeline\n\n```\n+-------------------------------------------------------------------+\n|                      DATA GENERATION PIPELINE                      |\n+-------------------------------------------------------------------+\n\n+------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+\n|   Generator      | --> |   State Store    | --> |   Render Layer   |\n|   (mock-data.ts) |     |   (canvasStore)  |     |   (LOD Layers)   |\n+--------+---------+     +--------+---------+     +--------+---------+\n         |                        |                        |\n         v                        v                        v\n+------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+\n| Persistent Types |     | Runtime State    |     | GPU Buffers      |\n| - Train          |     | - ViewportState  |     | - InstanceMatrix |\n| - Task           |     | - Interaction    |     | - InstanceColor  |\n| - DatasetMeta    |     | - EntityStore    |     | - HTML overlays  |\n+------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+\n```\n\n### Viewport Culling Flow\n\n```\n1. User pans/zooms canvas\n        |\n        v\n2. Camera position updates in state\n        |\n        v\n3. Calculate viewport bounds in world coordinates\n        |\n        v\n4. Query RBush spatial index (O(log n + k))\n        |\n        v\n5. Map IDs to TaskRender objects\n        |\n        v\n6. Update InstancedMesh:\n   mesh.count = visibleTasks.length\n   for each task: setMatrixAt, setColorAt\n   mesh.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate = true\n```\n\n---\n\n# PART III: USER EXPERIENCE\n\n## 15. Semantic Zoom Concept\n\n### Core Metaphor\n\n**\"Google Earth for Time\"** - The visualization transforms based on zoom level.\n\n```\n   ZOOM OUT (Macro)              ZOOM LEVEL               ZOOM IN (Micro)\n        <---------------------------------------------------------------->\n\n   +-------------+          +-------------+          +-------------+\n   | ░░▓▓░░░░░░ |          | ▓▓▓ ░░░ ▓▓ |          | +--------+ |\n   | ░░░▓▓▓░░░░ |          | ░░ ▓▓▓ ░░░ |          | | Task A | |\n   | ░▓▓▓░░░░░░ |          | ▓▓ ░░░ ▓▓▓ |          | | 8h     | |\n   +-------------+          +-------------+          +-------------+\n\n   12-YEAR HEATMAP          2-YEAR DNA STRIP        4-WEEK GANTT\n   (Strategic)               (Tactical)              (Operational)\n```\n\n| Zoom Level | Data Representation | User Question |\n|------------|---------------------|---------------|\n| **MACRO** | Aggregated density | \"Where are bottlenecks?\" |\n| **MESO** | Task blocks | \"What's the maintenance rhythm?\" |\n| **MICRO** | Detailed cards | \"What's this week's work?\" |\n\n### Continuous Navigation\n\n| Gesture | Action | Effect |\n|---------|--------|--------|\n| **Drag** | Pan canvas | Navigate through time and trains |\n| **Scroll wheel** | Zoom at cursor | Increase/decrease detail |\n| **Pinch** | Zoom at center | Two-finger zoom |\n| **Double-click** | Zoom in 2x | Quick zoom to point |\n\n---\n\n## 16. Visual Design System\n\n### 16.1 Color Palette\n\n**Primary Colors:**\n\n| Usage | Color | Hex |\n|-------|-------|-----|\n| Background | Zinc 100 | `#F4F4F5` |\n| Surface | White | `#FFFFFF` |\n| Text Primary | Gray 900 | `#111827` |\n| Text Secondary | Gray 500 | `#6B7280` |\n\n**Task Type Colors:**\n\n| Type | Color | Hex |\n|------|-------|-----|\n| Maintenance | Blue 700 | `#1D4ED8` |\n| Overhaul | Amber 500 | `#F59E0B` |\n| Inspection | Gray 500 | `#6B7280` |\n\n**Status Colors:**\n\n| Status | Color | Hex |\n|--------|-------|-----|\n| Conflict | Red 500 | `#EF4444` |\n| Selected | Cyan 500 | `#06B6D4` |\n| Focused | Violet 500 | `#8B5CF6` |\n| Success | Green 500 | `#22C55E` |\n| Warning | Yellow 500 | `#EAB308` |\n\n**Heatmap Gradient:**\n\n```\nLow Density ---------------------------------> High Density\n\n   #FFF3C4      #FDE68A      #FB923C      #EA580C      #DC2626\n   (Yellow)    (Lt Orange)  (Orange)    (Dp Orange)   (Red)\n      |            |            |            |            |\n   0-20%       20-40%       40-60%       60-80%       80-100%\n```\n\n### 16.2 Typography\n\n| Element | Font | Size | Weight |\n|---------|------|------|--------|\n| App Title | System sans-serif | 18px | Bold (700) |\n| Section Headers | System sans-serif | 14px | Semibold (600) |\n| Task Labels | System sans-serif | 11px | Regular (400) |\n| Timeline Dates | System sans-serif | 12px | Semibold (600) |\n| Train IDs | System monospace | 12px | Regular (400) |\n| Tooltips | System sans-serif | 13px | Regular (400) |\n\n---\n\n## 17. Layout Structure\n\n```\n+--------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n|                               NAVBAR                                      |\n|  +----------------------------------------------------------------------+|\n|  | Metro Integrated Planning System    | Fleet: 98% | [Zoom: 1.00x]    ||\n|  +----------------------------------------------------------------------+|\n+----------+---------------------------------------------------------------+\n|          |                    TIMELINE HEADER                             |\n|          |  +----------------------------------------------------------+  |\n|  SIDEBAR |  | 2024    2025    2026    2027    2028    2029    2030    |  |\n|          |  +----------------------------------------------------------+  |\n| +------+ +---------------------------------------------------------------+\n| | T001 | |                                                               |\n| | T002 | |                     CANVAS AREA                               |\n| | T003 | |                                                               |\n| | T004 | |          (React Three Fiber + InstancedMesh)                 |\n| | T005 | |                                                    +---+      |\n| | ...  | |                                                    | + | Zoom |\n| | T050 | |   +--------+                                       +---+      |\n| +------+ |   |MINIMAP |                                       | - |      |\n|          |   +--------+                                       +---+      |\n+----------+---------------------------------------------------------------+\n|                              TIME SCRUBBER                                |\n|  +----------------------------------------------------------------------+|\n|  | [=====================================O=============================] ||\n|  +----------------------------------------------------------------------+|\n+--------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n```\n\n### Component Dimensions\n\n| Component | Width | Height |\n|-----------|-------|--------|\n| Navbar | 100% | 48px |\n| Sidebar | 80px | calc(100vh - 96px) |\n| Timeline | calc(100% - 80px) | 32px |\n| Canvas | calc(100% - 80px) | Remaining |\n| Minimap | 120px | 80px |\n| Zoom Controls | 40px | 80px |\n| Time Scrubber | 100% | 48px |\n\n---\n\n## 18. Interaction Patterns\n\n### 18.1 Pan Navigation\n\n```\nUser drags from A to B\n\n        A ----------------> B\n\nCanvas moves in OPPOSITE direction\n(content appears to move with drag)\n```\n\n**Constraints:**\n- Unconstrained within data bounds\n- Soft-stop at edges with 10% overscan\n- Snap-back animation if dragged beyond\n\n### 18.2 Zoom Interaction\n\n```\nScroll UP (away from user)    ->    Zoom IN (more detail)\nScroll DOWN (toward user)     ->    Zoom OUT (less detail)\n\nZoom anchored at cursor position\n```\n\n**Zoom Range:**\n\n| Constant | Value | Purpose |\n|----------|-------|---------|\n| MIN_ZOOM | 0.1 | 20 years visible |\n| MAX_ZOOM | 5.0 | 1 week visible |\n| DEFAULT_ZOOM | 1.0 | 5 years visible |\n\n### 18.3 Gesture Mapping\n\n| Gesture | Device | Action |\n|---------|--------|--------|\n| Left-drag | Mouse | Pan canvas |\n| Scroll wheel | Mouse | Zoom at cursor |\n| Two-finger scroll | Trackpad | Pan canvas |\n| Pinch | Trackpad/Touch | Zoom at center |\n| Ctrl + Scroll | All | Horizontal pan |\n| Shift + Scroll | All | Vertical pan |\n| Double-click | All | Zoom in 2x |\n\n### 18.4 Momentum Physics\n\n```typescript\nconst FRICTION = 0.93;\nconst MIN_VELOCITY = 0.5;\n\nfunction startMomentum(initialVelocity: { x: number; y: number }) {\n  let velocity = { ...initialVelocity };\n\n  function animate() {\n    velocity.x *= FRICTION;\n    velocity.y *= FRICTION;\n\n    pan(velocity.x, velocity.y);\n\n    const magnitude = Math.sqrt(velocity.x ** 2 + velocity.y ** 2);\n    if (magnitude > MIN_VELOCITY) {\n      requestAnimationFrame(animate);\n    }\n  }\n\n  requestAnimationFrame(animate);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 19. Task Interaction States\n\n| State | Visual | Trigger | Exit |\n|-------|--------|---------|------|\n| **Normal** | Base color | Default | Any other state |\n| **Hover** | Brightness +30% | Mouse over | Mouse leave |\n| **Selected** | Cyan border (2px) | Click | Click elsewhere / Escape |\n| **Focused** | Violet ring (3px) | Tab navigation | Tab away |\n| **Conflict** | Red background | hasConflict: true | Data change |\n\n### Selection Flow\n\n```\n         click(task)\n              |\n              v\n       +------------+     click(other) / Escape\n       |  SELECTED  |------------------------+\n       +-----+------+                        |\n             |                               |\n             | click(task)                   |\n             v                               |\n       +------------+                        |\n       |  DETAILS   |------------------------+\n       |   PANEL    |\n       +------------+\n```\n\n---\n\n## 20. Keyboard Navigation\n\n| Key | Action |\n|-----|--------|\n| Tab | Focus next task |\n| Shift + Tab | Focus previous task |\n| Enter / Space | Select focused task |\n| Arrow Up/Down/Left/Right | Pan viewport |\n| +/- | Zoom in/out |\n| Escape | Clear selection / Close panel |\n| Home | Reset view to default |\n| End | Go to end of timeline |\n| Page Up/Down | Jump 1 year back/forward |\n\n---\n\n## 21. Accessibility\n\n### ARIA Implementation\n\n```tsx\n<div\n  role=\"application\"\n  aria-label=\"Maintenance planning canvas. Currently showing MESO view.\"\n  aria-roledescription=\"Interactive timeline canvas\"\n  tabIndex={0}\n>\n  <Canvas />\n  <div role=\"status\" aria-live=\"polite\" className=\"sr-only\">\n    {announcement}\n  </div>\n</div>\n```\n\n### Screen Reader Announcements\n\n| Event | Announcement |\n|-------|--------------|\n| LOD Change | \"View changed to Strategic heatmap / Tactical blocks / Operational details\" |\n| Zoom Change | \"75 percent zoom\" (debounced) |\n| Task Focus | \"Maintenance task, Train 15, Day 45, 3 days duration\" |\n| Task Selection | \"Task selected. Press Escape to deselect.\" |\n\n### Color Contrast (WCAG 2.1 AA)\n\n| Element | Foreground | Background | Ratio |\n|---------|------------|------------|-------|\n| Primary text | #111827 | #FFFFFF | 15.9:1 |\n| Secondary text | #6B7280 | #FFFFFF | 4.9:1 |\n| Task labels | #FFFFFF | #1D4ED8 | 7.1:1 |\n\n### Reduced Motion\n\n```css\n@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {\n  * {\n    animation-duration: 0.001ms !important;\n    transition-duration: 0.001ms !important;\n  }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 22. UI Components\n\n### Navbar\n\n```tsx\n<Navbar>\n  <NavbarGroup align=\"left\">\n    <NavbarHeading>\n      <Icon icon=\"train\" />\n      Metro Integrated Planning System\n    </NavbarHeading>\n  </NavbarGroup>\n  <NavbarGroup align=\"right\">\n    <Tag intent=\"success\">Fleet: 98%</Tag>\n    <Tag large>[{lodLevel}] {zoomPercent}%</Tag>\n    <Button icon=\"refresh\" minimal onClick={resetView} />\n  </NavbarGroup>\n</Navbar>\n```\n\n### Train Sidebar\n\n```tsx\n<Card className=\"train-sidebar\">\n  <div className=\"sidebar-header\">\n    <H5>Fleet</H5>\n    <Tag minimal>50 trains</Tag>\n  </div>\n  <div\n    className=\"train-list\"\n    style={{ transform: `translateY(-${scrollY}px)` }}\n  >\n    {trains.map(train => (\n      <div key={train.id} className=\"train-row\">\n        <span className=\"train-id\">{train.id}</span>\n      </div>\n    ))}\n  </div>\n</Card>\n```\n\n### Zoom Controls\n\n```tsx\n<ButtonGroup vertical className=\"zoom-controls\">\n  <Tooltip content=\"Zoom In (+)\">\n    <Button icon=\"plus\" onClick={() => zoomBy(1.5)} disabled={zoom >= MAX_ZOOM} />\n  </Tooltip>\n  <Tooltip content=\"Zoom Out (-)\">\n    <Button icon=\"minus\" onClick={() => zoomBy(0.67)} disabled={zoom <= MIN_ZOOM} />\n  </Tooltip>\n  <Tooltip content=\"Reset View (Home)\">\n    <Button icon=\"home\" onClick={resetView} />\n  </Tooltip>\n</ButtonGroup>\n```\n\n### LOD Indicator\n\n```tsx\nfunction LODIndicator({ lod, zoom }) {\n  const intentMap = { MACRO: 'warning', MESO: 'primary', MICRO: 'success' };\n  const labelMap = { MACRO: 'Strategic', MESO: 'Tactical', MICRO: 'Operational' };\n\n  return (\n    <Tag intent={intentMap[lod]} large round>\n      {labelMap[lod]} - {Math.round(zoom * 100)}%\n    </Tag>\n  );\n}\n```\n\n### Timeline Header\n\n**Adaptive Label Format:**\n\n| LOD Level | Visible Range | Label Format |\n|-----------|---------------|--------------|\n| MACRO | 10+ years | \"2028\" |\n| MESO | 2-5 years | \"Oct 2028\" |\n| MICRO | 4-8 weeks | \"19 Oct\" |\n\n---\n\n## 23. Conflict Visualization\n\n### Conflict Types (V1.0)\n\n| Type | Visual | Description |\n|------|--------|-------------|\n| Task Overlap | Red background | Two tasks on same train at same time |\n\n### Conflict Indication\n\n**MESO Layer:**\n- Normal task: Blue fill\n- Conflict task: Red fill + thicker border\n\n**MICRO Layer:**\n- Red border on task card\n- Warning icon in top-right\n\n### Conflict Colors\n\n```typescript\nconst CONFLICT_COLORS = {\n  background: '#FEE2E2',  // Red 100\n  border: '#EF4444',      // Red 500\n  icon: '#DC2626',        // Red 600\n  text: '#991B1B',        // Red 800\n};\n```\n\n---\n\n## 24. Demo Flow\n\n### 12-Minute Demo Script\n\n**[0:00-1:00] Opening - Strategic Overview**\n- Show full MACRO heatmap (50 trains, 12 years)\n- Point to color gradient, identify red hotspot in Q3 2028\n- **WOW:** All 60,000 tasks visible at 60fps\n\n**[1:00-3:00] MACRO to MESO Transition**\n- Zoom into bottleneck\n- Watch heatmap dissolve into DNA pattern\n- **WOW:** Seamless crossfade, no mode switch\n\n**[3:00-5:00] MESO Analysis**\n- Pan across timeline\n- Hover over tasks for tooltips\n- **WOW:** Diagonal DNA pattern = balanced workload\n\n**[5:00-7:00] Conflict Detection**\n- Navigate to red tasks\n- Explain conflict indicators\n- **WOW:** Instant visibility across entire fleet\n\n**[7:00-9:00] MESO to MICRO Transition**\n- Continue zooming\n- Watch blocks become cards\n- **WOW:** Labels emerge naturally\n\n**[9:00-10:30] MICRO Analysis**\n- Click task for selection\n- Show task details panel\n- **WOW:** Full operational visibility\n\n**[10:30-11:30] Quick Navigation**\n- Press Home to reset\n- Use minimap and time scrubber\n- **WOW:** Instant navigation, no lag\n\n**[11:30-12:00] Closing**\n- One final zoom cycle\n- Key takeaways: 12-year visibility, instant drill-down, conflict detection\n\n### Demo Checkpoints\n\n- [ ] 60fps during pan/zoom\n- [ ] All 3 LOD levels render\n- [ ] Smooth transitions\n- [ ] Conflict tasks visible (red)\n- [ ] Sidebar synced with canvas\n- [ ] Timeline shows dates\n- [ ] Zoom controls functional\n- [ ] No console errors\n\n---\n\n## 25. Browser Support\n\n### Target Browsers (V1.0)\n\n| Browser | Version | Support |\n|---------|---------|---------|\n| Chrome | 100+ | Full (Primary) |\n| Edge | 100+ | Full |\n| Firefox | 100+ | Full |\n| Safari | 15+ | Full |\n\n### Not Supported\n\n| Browser | Reason |\n|---------|--------|\n| Internet Explorer | No WebGL2 |\n| Opera Mini | No WebGL |\n| Mobile browsers | Deferred to V1.1 |\n\n---\n\n# APPENDICES\n\n## Appendix A: Decision Log\n\n| ID | Area | Decision | Rationale |\n|----|------|----------|-----------|\n| DECISION-001 | Solver | Defer to V1.1 | Demo is visualization, solver adds backend complexity |\n| DECISION-002 | UI Framework | Keep BlueprintJS 6 | Working, cosmetic change not worth risk |\n| DECISION-003 | Multi-Depot | Include in data model | Client has 2 depots, visual only |\n| DECISION-004 | Km Variance | Use 10%/15% thresholds | Conservative values from domain docs |\n| DECISION-005 | Audit Trail | Defer to V1.1 | Requires backend, not demo differentiator |\n| DECISION-006 | Cascade Display | Data only, no visual | Complex UI, data model sufficient |\n| DECISION-007 | InstancedMesh | Pre-allocate 100k | Avoid GPU buffer reallocation |\n| DECISION-008 | Viewport Query | RBush MANDATORY | 100x faster than Array.filter |\n| DECISION-009 | GC Prevention | Object pooling | GC is primary bottleneck |\n| DECISION-010 | MicroLayer | Max 50 labels | HTML is expensive |\n| DECISION-011 | LOD Transition | depthWrite: false | Prevent z-fighting |\n| DECISION-012 | Timeline | 3 weeks to demo | Balance performance and features |\n\n---\n\n## Appendix B: Glossary\n\n### Railway Domain Terms\n\n| Term | Definition |\n|------|------------|\n| **CBM** | Condition-Based Maintenance |\n| **Dead Running** | Non-revenue movement of rolling stock |\n| **Depot** | Designated maintenance facility |\n| **ECM** | Entity in Charge of Maintenance (EU regulatory framework) |\n| **Fleet** | Group of rolling stock sharing manufacturer/model |\n| **Mileage Balancing** | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet |\n| **RAMS** | Reliability, Availability, Maintainability, Safety |\n| **Rolling Stock** | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance |\n| **Run Assignment** | Daily allocation of train IDs to scheduled services |\n| **Train Fitness** | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) |\n\n### Maintenance Terms\n\n| Term | Definition |\n|------|------------|\n| **HM** | Heavy Maintenance |\n| **MDBF** | Mean Distance Between Failures |\n| **MTBF** | Mean Time Between Failures |\n| **MTTR** | Mean Time To Repair |\n| **OVH** | Overhaul (resets counters) |\n| **PM1/PM2/PM3** | Preventive Maintenance levels 1, 2, 3 |\n\n### Technical Terms\n\n| Term | Definition |\n|------|------------|\n| **CP** | Constraint Programming |\n| **LOD** | Level of Detail |\n| **MIP** | Mixed Integer Programming |\n| **SoA** | Structure of Arrays (data layout) |\n\n### MMS-Specific Terms\n\n| Term | Definition |\n|------|------------|\n| **Blend Zone** | Zoom range where two LOD layers crossfade |\n| **DNA Pattern** | Visual pattern of diagonal task stripes |\n| **MACRO Layer** | Heatmap view at zoom < 0.5x |\n| **MESO Layer** | Task block view at zoom 0.5x - 2.0x |\n| **MICRO Layer** | Detailed card view at zoom > 2.0x |\n| **Semantic Zoom** | Google Earth-style LOD switching |\n| **TaskRender** | Computed task data for canvas rendering |\n\n---\n\n## Document History\n\n| Version | Date | Author | Changes |\n|---------|------|--------|---------|\n| 1.0.0 | 2026-01-06 | MMS V1.0 GENESIS | Initial consolidated specification |\n\n---\n\n## References\n\n### Standards\n\n- EU Regulation 2019/779 - ECM Certification\n- EN 50126-1:2017 - Railway RAMS\n- EN 15380 Series - Railway Classification System\n- IRS 30100-3 - Maintenance Terms\n- IRS 70711 - Rolling Stock Maintenance\n\n---\n\n*END OF MMS V1.0 SPECIFICATION*\n*Total Lines: ~2800*\n*Last Updated: FIXED**\n   597→\n   598→Problem: Trackpad pinch-to-zoom on MacBook had ~300ms latency causing perceived lag.\n   599→\n   600→Root Cause:\n   601→- 50ms accumulation timeout to batch wheel events\n   602→- 250ms animation duration per zoom operation\n   603→- Multiple rapid pinches queued animations\n   604→\n   605→Fix:\n   606→- Modified `handleWheel` ctrlKey branch to apply zoom DIRECTLY\n   607→- Removed accumulation timeout for trackpad pinch events\n   608→- Removed animation delay - zoom applied immediately like `handlePinchZoom`\n   609→- Cursor-centered zoom preserved (world point stays fixed)\n   610→\n   611→Also refactored handleWheel to Google Maps-like behavior:\n   612→- 2-finger scroll = pan (deltaX + deltaY)\n   613→- Ctrl+scroll (pinch) = zoom toward cursor\n   614→\n   615→**Files Changed:**\n   616→- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` - Sync Loop Scheduler:** Fixed execution order, physics/render decoupled\n   649→5. **Phase 5 - Object Pooling & GC Elimination:** Closures, for loops, pre-compute\n   650→6. **Phase 6 - Mobile Optimizations:** Passive listeners, iOS limits (256MB/380MB)\n   651→7. **Phase 7 - Undo/Redo:** Command Pattern + Immer patches\n   652→8. **Phase 8 - Worker Layer:** Optional, OffscreenCanvas + SharedArrayBuffer\n   653→\n   654→**Critical Decision: @zazcart/tools over Zustand**\n   655→\n   656→PDF recommends Zustand, but this project uses **@zazcart/tools** exclusively:\n   657→- Documented in 3 places with \"NUNCA Zustand\" warning\n   658→- Clarifications added to phases explaining Simulation vs UI state separation\n   659→- @zazcart/tools is ONLY for UI state (<30fps, event-driven)\n   660→- Simulation State uses TypedArrays with direct access (60fps)\n   661→\n   662→**Verification Completed:**\n   663→- All 11 PDF sections mapped to implementation phases\n   664→- All recommendations from Sections 2-9 covered\n   665→- Risk matrix mitigations included\n   666→- Performance targets defined (100k entities, 60fps, <100MB, <5ms queries)\n   667→\n   668→**Files Changed:**\n   669→- `features.json` - PLANNED\n   679→### BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance - IDENTIFIED\n   680→\n   681→**Objective:**\n   682→Deep research and planning for viewport culling optimization + Investigation of trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook.\n   683→\n   684→**Research Conducted:**\n   685→- Web searches: Three.js frustum culling, R3F viewport culling, RBush spatial indexing, deck.gl massive rendering, Figma WebGL architecture\n   686→- Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)\n   687→- Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant)\n   688→- Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation)\n   689→\n   690→**Key Findings:**\n   691→\n   692→1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport\n   693→   - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame\n   694→   - No built-in per-instance frustum culling\n   695→\n   696→2. **RBush Spatial Index:**\n   697→   - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time)\n   698→   - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms\n   699→   - Perfect for viewport culling queries\n   700→\n   701→3. **Culling Strategy:**\n   702→   - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer\n   703→   - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered)\n   704→   - No new allocations during pan/zoom\n   705→\n   706→4. **Alternative Libraries:**\n   707→   - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling\n   708→   - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting\n   709→   - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data\n   710→\n   711→**Documents Created:**\n   712→- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Comprehensive research consolidating all findings\n   713→\n   714→**Task Created: PERF-002**\n   715→\n   716→Added to features.json with 4 implementation phases:\n   717→\n   718→1. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree for task/entity lookup\n   719→2. **Phase 2 - Culled InstancedMesh:** Instance reordering + count manipulation\n   720→3. **Phase 3 - Layer Integration:** MesoLayer, MacroLayer viewport-aware\n   721→4. **Phase 4 - Validation:** Performance testing and profiling\n   722→\n   723→**Dependency Update:**\n   724→- STATE-ARCH-001 now `blockedBy: \"PERF-002\"`\n   725→- PERF-002 is incremental improvement that can be done BEFORE full architecture migration\n   726→- Establishes viewport culling foundation that STATE-ARCH-001 will build upon\n   727→\n   728→**Performance Targets:**\n   729→- Viewport query: <5ms for 1000 results\n   730→- Instance update: <1ms for 1000 visible\n   731→- FPS: 60fps constant during pan/zoom\n   732→- GPU instances: Only visible count (not all 60k)\n   733→\n   734→**BUG-009 Investigation (Added):**\n   735→- User reported: Trackpad zoom has lag/stutter, but minimap navigation is smooth\n   736→- Diagnosis: This is INPUT HANDLING issue, not rendering (minimap proves rendering is fine)\n   737→- Root cause candidates:\n   738→  - ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms creates queued animations\n   739→  - ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms delays execution\n   740→  - animateZoomTo() starts new RAF instead of updating target\n   741→- Solution options:\n   742→  - Option A: Remove animation for trackpad (direct zoom like minimap)\n   743→  - Option B: Update target during animation\n   744→  - Option C: Use d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react (market solutions)\n   745→\n   746→**Files Changed:**\n   747→- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` (NEW - comprehensive research)\n   748→- `features.json`:\n   749→  - Added PERF-002 with 5 phases (phase0 = BUG-009 fix first)\n   750→  - Added BUG-009 with detailed investigation\n   751→  - Updated STATE-ARCH-001 blockedBy: PERF-002\n   752→- `claude-progress.txt` (this file)\n   753→\n   754→**Alignment Fix (Added):**\n   755→- PERF-002.Phase1 was duplicating STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 (both had RBush)\n   756→- Fix: PERF-002.Phase1 now defines ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only + placeholder\n   757→- STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays\n   758→- No more duplicate work - tasks are now complementary\n   759→\n   760→**Status:** Planning complete. Tasks aligned. Ready for implementation.\n   761→\n   762→---\n   763→\n   764→## Session: 2025-12-17 (STATE-ARCH-001 Planning) - COMPLETE ✅\n   765→\n   766→### STATE-ARCH-001: High-Performance State Management Architecture - PLANNED\n   767→\n   768→**Objective:**\n   769→Plan refactoring of state management architecture based on research document analyzing Figma, Google Maps, CesiumJS, and WebGL game engines.\n   770→\n   771→**Research Document:**\n   772→- `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf`\n   773→- Key finding: GC (Garbage Collection) is the primary bottleneck, not rendering\n   774→- React Hooks and reactive stores inadequate for 60fps on mobile\n   775→- Recommends Dual-Store: Simulation State (TypedArrays) + Application State (external store)\n   776→\n   777→**Task Created: STATE-ARCH-001**\n   778→\n   779→Added comprehensive task to features.json with 5 implementation phases:\n   780→\n   781→1. **Phase 1 - Object Pooling:** Eliminate GC pauses in animation loops\n   785→5. **Phase 5 - Undo/Redo:** Command Pattern + Immer patches\n   786→\n   787→**CRITICAL DECISION: @zazcart/tools over Zustand**\n   788→\n   789→The PDF recommends Zustand for UI state, but this project uses **@zazcart/tools** (packages/toolkit):\n   790→- Already implemented and integrated\n   791→- Uses Immer for immutability\n   792→- Uses useSyncExternalStore for synchronous updates\n   793→- Has slice-based architecture matching project needs\n   794→\n   795→**Files Changed:**\n   796→- `features.json` - Added STATE-ARCH-001 task with detailed implementation plan\n   797→- `CLAUDE.md` - Added \"High-Performance Architecture\" section with guidelines\n   798→\n   799→**Performance Targets:**\n   800→- 100,000+ entities renderable\n   801→- 60fps constant during pan/zoom\n   802→- <100MB memory for 100k entities\n   803→- <5ms viewport queries\n   804→- Zero perceptible GC pauses\n   805→\n   806→**Next Steps:**\n   807→- Implementation will be phased over multiple sessions\n   808→- Start with Phase 1 (EntityStore) when prioritized\n   809→\n   810→---\n   811→\n   812→## Session: 2025-12-17 (UX Bug Fixes) - COMPLETE ✅\n\n### D3 Modules Integration Analysis - COMPLETE\n\n**Objective:**\nComprehensive codebase analysis to identify opportunities for integrating D3.js modules to replace ad-hoc implementations, improving cross-browser compatibility, performance, and code quality.\n\n**Analysis Scope:**\n- 19 TypeScript files in `apps/canvas/src/` analyzed\n- Used 3 parallel Explore agents for efficient discovery\n- Full planning mode with deep thinking\n\n**Key Findings:**\n\n| Module | Purpose | Size (gzip) | Files Affected |\n|--------|---------|-------------|----------------|\n| d3-zoom | Pan/zoom/gestures | ~8KB | InfiniteCanvas.tsx |\n| d3-scale | Linear/time scales | ~6KB | Minimap.tsx, TimelineHeader.tsx |\n| d3-array | bin, rollup, extent | ~4KB | MacroLayer.tsx, Minimap.tsx, types.ts |\n| d3-time | Date arithmetic | ~3KB | TimelineHeader.tsx, generator.ts |\n| d3-time-format | Date formatting | ~2KB | TimelineHeader.tsx |\n| d3-interpolate | Color interpolation | ~3KB | MacroLayer.tsx, Minimap.tsx |\n\n**Estimated Impact:**\n- ~300 lines of code can be replaced\n- ~26KB bundle increase (tree-shakeable)\n- BUG-009 potentially resolved via d3-zoom\n- 60-70% reduction in event handling complexity\n\n**Files with D3 Opportunities:**\n1. **InfiniteCanvas.tsx (HIGH)** - d3-zoom for normalizeWheelDelta, trackpad detection, pinch\n2. **TimelineHeader.tsx (MEDIUM)** - d3-time, d3-time-format, d3-scale\n3. **Minimap.tsx (MEDIUM)** - d3-scale, d3-interpolate\n4. **MacroLayer.tsx (LOW)** - d3-scale-chromatic, d3-array\n5. **types.ts (LOW)** - d3-array (extent)\n6. **generator.ts (OPTIONAL)** - d3-time (not critical)\n\n**Files WITHOUT D3 Opportunities:**\n- viewport.ts (architecture sound with @zazcart/tools)\n- App.tsx (orchestration layer)\n- MesoLayer.tsx (R3F-specific)\n- MicroLayer.tsx (React/CSS domain)\n- PerfMonitor.tsx, TaskTooltip.tsx, TaskDetailsPanel.tsx (UI components)\n\n**Phased Migration Plan:**\n- **Phase 1 (Quick Wins):** types.ts, TimelineHeader.tsx, Minimap.tsx\n- **Phase 2 (Core Optimization):** InfiniteCanvas.tsx with d3-zoom (R3F adapter)\n- **Phase 3 (Visual Polish):** MacroLayer.tsx heatmap\n\n**Output Files Created:**\n1. `apps/canvas/d3-opportunities.txt` - Gradient overlays (30px width) covered labels due to missing z-index\n2. PerformancePanel positioned at `top: 50` overlapped TimelineHeader\n\nFix:\n- Added `zIndex: 2` to labels and background color for readability\n- Reduced gradient overlay width from 30px to 20px with `zIndex: 1`\n- Moved PerformancePanel from \"top-left\" to \"bottom-left\" position\n\n**BUG-009: Trackpad Zoom Performance Issue - FIXED**\n\nProblem: Trackpad pinch-to-zoom on MacBook had ~300ms latency causing perceived lag.\n\nRoot Cause:\n- 50ms accumulation timeout to batch wheel events\n- 250ms animation duration per zoom operation\n- Multiple rapid pinches queued animations\n\nFix:\n- Modified `handleWheel` ctrlKey branch to apply zoom DIRECTLY\n- Removed accumulation timeout for trackpad pinch events\n- Removed animation delay - zoom applied immediately like `handlePinchZoom`\n- Cursor-centered zoom preserved (world point stays fixed)\n\nAlso refactored handleWheel to Google Maps-like behavior:\n- 2-finger scroll = pan (deltaX + deltaY)\n- Ctrl+scroll (pinch) = zoom toward cursor\n\n**Files Changed:**\n- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` - Undo/Redo:** Command Pattern + Immer patches\n8. **Phase 8 - Worker Layer:** Optional, OffscreenCanvas + SharedArrayBuffer\n\n**Critical Decision: @zazcart/tools over Zustand**\n\nPDF recommends Zustand, but this project uses **@zazcart/tools** exclusively:\n- Documented in 3 places with \"NUNCA Zustand\" warning\n- Clarifications added to phases explaining Simulation vs UI state separation\n- @zazcart/tools is ONLY for UI state (<30fps, event-driven)\n- Simulation State uses TypedArrays with direct access (60fps)\n\n**Verification Completed:**\n- All 11 PDF sections mapped to implementation phases\n- All recommendations from Sections 2-9 covered\n- Risk matrix mitigations included\n- Performance targets defined (100k entities, 60fps, <100MB, <5ms queries)\n\n**Files Changed:**\n- `features.json` -
