IDENTIFIED\n\n**Objective:**\nDeep research and planning for viewport culling optimization + Investigation of trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook.\n\n**Research Conducted:**\n- Web searches: Three.js frustum culling, R3F viewport culling, RBush spatial indexing, deck.gl massive rendering, Figma WebGL architecture\n- Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)\n- Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant)\n- Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation)\n\n**Key Findings:**\n\n1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport\n   - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame\n   - No built-in per-instance frustum culling\n\n2. **RBush Spatial Index:**\n   - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time)\n   - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms\n   - Perfect for viewport culling queries\n\n3. **Culling Strategy:**\n   - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer\n   - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered)\n   - No new allocations during pan/zoom\n\n4. **Alternative Libraries:**\n   - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling\n   - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting\n   - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data\n\n**Documents Created:**\n- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Comprehensive research consolidating all findings\n\n**Task Created: PERF-002**\n\nAdded to features.json with 4 implementation phases:\n\n1. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree for task/entity lookup\n2. **Phase 2 - Culled InstancedMesh:** Instance reordering + count manipulation\n3. **Phase 3 - Layer Integration:** MesoLayer, MacroLayer viewport-aware\n4. **Phase 4 - Validation:** Performance testing and profiling\n\n**Dependency Update:**\n- STATE-ARCH-001 now `blockedBy: \"PERF-002\"`\n- PERF-002 is incremental improvement that can be done BEFORE full architecture migration\n- Establishes viewport culling foundation that STATE-ARCH-001 will build upon\n\n**Performance Targets:**\n- Viewport query: <5ms for 1000 results\n- Instance update: <1ms for 1000 visible\n- FPS: 60fps constant during pan/zoom\n- GPU instances: Only visible count (not all 60k)\n\n**BUG-009 Investigation (Added):**\n- User reported: Trackpad zoom has lag/stutter, but minimap navigation is smooth\n- Diagnosis: This is INPUT HANDLING issue, not rendering (minimap proves rendering is fine)\n- Root cause candidates:\n  - ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms creates queued animations\n  - ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms delays execution\n  - animateZoomTo() starts new RAF instead of updating target\n- Solution options:\n  - Option A: Remove animation for trackpad (direct zoom like minimap)\n  - Option B: Update target during animation\n  - Option C: Use d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react (market solutions)\n\n**Files Changed:**\n- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` (NEW - comprehensive research)\n- `features.json`:\n  - Added PERF-002 with 5 phases (phase0 = BUG-009 fix first)\n  - Added BUG-009 with detailed investigation\n  - Updated STATE-ARCH-001 blockedBy: PERF-002\n- `claude-progress.txt` (this file)\n\n**Alignment Fix (Added):**\n- PERF-002.Phase1 was duplicating STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 (both had RBush)\n- Fix: PERF-002.Phase1 now defines ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only + placeholder\n- STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays\n- No more duplicate work - tasks are now complementary\n\n**Status:** Planning complete. Tasks aligned. Ready for implementation.\n\n---\n\n## Session: 2025-12-17 (STATE-ARCH-001 Planning) - COMPLETE ✅\n\n### STATE-ARCH-001: High-Performance State Management Architecture - PLANNED\n\n**Objective:**\nPlan refactoring of state management architecture based on research document analyzing Figma, Google Maps, CesiumJS, and WebGL game engines.\n\n**Research Document:**\n- `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf`\n- Key finding: GC (Garbage Collection) is the primary bottleneck, not rendering\n- React Hooks and reactive stores inadequate for 60fps on mobile\n- Recommends Dual-Store: Simulation State (TypedArrays) + Application State (external store)\n\n**Task Created: STATE-ARCH-001**\n\nAdded comprehensive task to features.json with 5 implementation phases:\n\n1. **Phase 1 - Foundation:** Create EntityStore with TypedArrays (Structure of Arrays pattern)\n2. **Phase 2 - Spatial Index:** Implement RBush R-tree for viewport queries\n3. **Phase 3 - UI Bridge:** Connect Engine State to @zazcart/tools (NOT Zustand as PDF suggests)\n4. **Phase 4 - Object Pooling:** Eliminate GC pauses in animation loops\n5. **Phase 5 - Undo/Redo:** Command Pattern + Immer patches\n\n**CRITICAL DECISION: @zazcart/tools over Zustand**\n\nThe PDF recommends Zustand for UI state, but this project uses **@zazcart/tools** (packages/toolkit):\n- Already implemented and integrated\n- Uses Immer for immutability\n- Uses useSyncExternalStore for synchronous updates\n- Has slice-based architecture matching project needs\n\n**Files Changed:**\n- `features.json` - Added STATE-ARCH-001 task with detailed implementation plan\n- `CLAUDE.md` - Added \"High-Performance Architecture\" section with guidelines\n\n**Performance Targets:**\n- 100,000+ entities renderable\n- 60fps constant during pan/zoom\n- <100MB memory for 100k entities\n- <5ms viewport queries\n- Zero perceptible GC pauses\n\n**Next Steps:**\n- Implementation will be phased over multiple sessions\n- Start with Phase 1 (EntityStore) when prioritized\n\n---\n\n## Session: 2025-12-17 (UX Bug Fixes) - Semantic Zoom as Differentiator**\nUnlike typical data visualization (D3.js, Plotly), MMS implements **Google Earth-style semantic zoom**:\n- Macro (< 0.5x) - 10-20 year strategic view with heatmap\n- Meso (0.5x-2.0x) - 2-5 year tactical view with task blocks\n- Micro (> 2.0x) - 4-8 week operational view with details + labels + dependencies\n\nEach layer has **completely different data representations** (not just zoom), matching user mental models.\n\n**Learning:** Semantic zoom requires fundamentally different rendering at each layer; direct z-scale zoom insufficient.\n\n#### **6. Theme variables and globals\n│   ├── components/\n│   │   ├── sidebar/FleetSidebar.tsx  # 128-train list with filters\n│   │   ├── panels/TaskDetailPanel.tsx # Task selection drawer\n│   │   └── canvas/\n│   │       ├── CanvasContainer.tsx   # R3F canvas wrapper\n│   │       └── RenderIndicator.tsx   # DOM verification component\n│   ├── render/\n│   │   ├── MacroLayer.tsx            # Heatmap visualization (LOD)\n│   │   ├── MesoLayer.tsx             # Test environment\n├── _memory/\n│   ├── ux-competitor-analysis.md     # IBM Maximo, Railnova, SignatureRail\n│   ├── ux-specification.md           # 2191-line UX bible\n│   ├── design-system.md              # Colors, typography, spacing\n│   ├── architecture-decisions.md     # ADR-001 through ADR-006+\n│   ├── code-patterns.md              # Working + anti-patterns\n│   ├── navigation-flows.md           # User journeys\n│   ├── ux-charts.md                  # Chart type specifications\n│   ├── ux-screens.md                 # Screen definitions\n│   ├── ux-unknown-unknowns.md        # 10 critical discoveries\n│   └── chart-types.md                # Heatmap, Gantt, Marey\n├── CLAUDE.md                          # Bootstrap instructions\n├── __brainstorming.md                # Cascade framework rules\n├── progress.txt                       # Harness state log\n├── features.json                      # Feature tracking (passes: true/false)\n├── package.json                       # React 19, PatternFly 6, R3F 9\n└── node_modules/                     # Installed dependencies\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. UX RESEARCH DOCUMENTS & FINDINGS\n\n### 2.1 Competitor Analysis (ux-competitor-analysis.md - 318 lines)\n\n**Three main competitors analyzed:**\n\n#### IBM Maximo Scheduler\n- **Strength**: Drag-drop Gantt + resource histogram (gold standard)\n- **Weakness**: Struggles at 10k+ records; discrete day/week/month tabs\n- **Steal**: Drag-drop capability, resource loading visualization\n\n#### Railnova (Brussels-based)\n- **Strength**: Event state pipeline (unplanned → planned → overdue → closed)\n- **Strength**: Digital train logbook for drivers/technicians\n- **Strength**: Color-coded anomaly detection\n- **Steal**: Event state machine, anomaly indicators, real-time monitoring\n\n#### SignatureRail (TrainPlan since 1994)\n- **Strength**: Marey chart visualization (industry gold standard since 1878)\n- **Strength**: What-if scenario management\n- **Marey advantage**: Superior time/space visualization for train scheduling\n- **Steal**: Marey chart implementation, scenario comparison\n\n**Gestalt Principles Applied:**\n- Proximity: Group related filters near views\n- Similarity: Consistent colors for data types\n- Enclosure: Cards with clear borders\n- Connectedness: Dependency lines between tasks\n- Closure: Minimal axis lines\n- Continuity: Chronological sorting\n\n**Differentiators:**\n| Feature | Competitors | MMS |\n|---------|-------------|-----|\n| Zoom | Discrete tabs | Continuous semantic zoom |\n| Performance | Struggles @ 10k | 60fps @ 60k tasks |\n| Interface | Fragmented modules | Unified canvas |\n| Visualization | Gantt only | Gantt + Marey + Heatmap (LOD) |\n\n### 2.2 Unknown Unknowns (ux-unknown-unknowns.md - 387 lines)\n\n**10 Critical Domain Discoveries:**\n\n1. **Statutory Tasks** (Non-moveable)\n   - Government-mandated safety inspections cannot be rescheduled\n   - UX: Lock icon + striped pattern\n   - Add to Task type: `isStatutory: boolean`\n\n2. **Depot Bay Limits** (Physical constraint)\n   - Al Rashidiya: 4-6 bays (49 Kinki Sharyo trains)\n   - Jebel Ali: 6-8 bays (79 Alstom trains)\n   - Cannot schedule more trains than bays available\n   - Need: Bay conflict detection (different from time conflicts)\n\n3. **Dependency Bottleneck** (Cascading changes)\n   - Moving one task can affect 12+ dependent tasks\n   - Performance: Cannot recalculate on every drag\n   - Solution: Debounced impact calculation (300ms), progressive disclosure\n\n4. **24-Hour Operation Reality**\n   - Maintenance windows: 5-6 hours/night (12:00am - 5:30am)\n   - Some special events: 24hr operation\n   - Need: Maintenance window visualization on timeline\n\n5. **Disruption Mode** (Disaster recovery)\n   - Dubai flooding (Apr 2024) closed stations for 38 days\n   - Need: Emergency schedule activation, backlog tracking, recovery planning\n\n6. **Standardized KPIs** (EN 15341 standard)\n   - MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures (higher = better)\n   - MTTR: Mean Time To Repair (lower = better)\n   - PM Compliance: > 95%\n   - Schedule Compliance: > 90%\n   - Need: KPI dashboard with trends and drill-down\n\n7. **Approval Workflows** (Multi-level)\n   - Depot Manager approves weekly schedule changes\n   - Operations Director approves overhauls\n   - Finance approves resource changes\n   - Need: Role-based access, approval queue, audit trail\n\n8. **Spare Parts Availability** (#1 delay cause)\n   - #1 reason tasks don't happen: parts unavailable\n   - Need: Parts status indicator (Green/Yellow/Red by ETA)\n   - Integration point: SAP/Maximo for stock levels\n\n9. **Mobile for Technicians**\n   - Floor-level staff need tablet interface (Railnova Digital Train Logbook pattern)\n   - \"My tasks today\" view\n   - Offline capability (caching)\n   - Photo upload for issues\n\n10. **Historical Data for Predictions**\n    - Track actual vs estimated duration\n    - Build ML model for future predictions\n    - Show confidence intervals (\"±30min confidence\")\n    - Anomaly detection (\"Train X needs 3x normal maintenance\")\n\n**Priority Matrix:**\n- **Must Have (Phase 1)**: Statutory locks, Bay capacity, KPIs, Impact preview\n- **Should Have (Phase 1.5)**: Maintenance windows, Approval workflow, Disruption mode\n- **Nice to Have (Phase 2)**: Parts availability, Mobile view, AI predictions\n\n### 2.3 Design System (design-system.md - 560 lines)\n\n**Color Palette (Dark Theme Primary):**\n- `--mms-bg-app`: #1a1a2e (main background)\n- `--mms-bg-card`: #16213e (card/panel)\n- `--mms-text-primary`: #f9fafb (near white)\n- `--canvas-bg`: #0A0A0A (pure black - OLED power savings)\n- Maintenance types: PM1 (green), PM2 (blue), HM (amber), OVH (red)\n- Status: Available (green), In-service (blue), Maintenance (red)\n- Variance: Green (good), Yellow (warning), Red (danger)\n\n**Typography:**\n- Primary font: Inter\n- Monospace: JetBrains Mono (train IDs, timestamps)\n- Sizes: 10px (xs) → 36px (4xl)\n- Weights: 500 (medium), 600 (semibold)\n\n**Spacing (4px base unit):**\n- `--mms-sidebar-width`: 130px (collapsed), 280px (expanded)\n- `--mms-navbar-height`: 44px\n- `--px-per-train`: 24px\n- `--px-per-day`: 2px\n- `--task-card-height`: 36px\n\n**LOD System:**\n```\nZoom:  0.05  0.35  0.50         2.00  2.15  5.0\nMACRO  [=====|-----|             |     |    ]\nMESO         [-----|=============|-----|    ]\nMICRO        |     [-----|=============|====]\n```\n- MACRO: < 0.5 zoom (heatmap, 100% opacity)\n- MESO: 0.5-2.0 zoom (DNA/bars, 100% opacity)\n- MICRO: > 2.0 zoom (detailed cards, 100% opacity)\n- Crossfade zones: 15% hysteresis overlap\n\n**Three-Layer Sandwich Architecture:**\n```\nLayer 3: HUD Overlay (HTML/React) - pointer-events: none\n  └─ Navbar, Sidebar, Timeline Header, Minimap, Tooltips\nLayer 2: Canvas (WebGL/R3F)\n  └─ Task bars, dependency lines, heatmaps, LOD layers\nLayer 1: Engine\n  └─ Constraint processing, data calculations\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. DESIGN DECISIONS & PATTERNS\n\n### 3.1 Architecture Decision Records (architecture-decisions.md)\n\n**ADR-001: PixiJS v8 for WebGL** (NOT in final!)\n- **Rejected**: DOM/SVG rendering inadequate for 500k+ data points\n- **Choice**: PixiJS v8 (most performant 2D WebGL engine)\n- **Note**: Later evolved to React Three Fiber in final implementation\n\n**ADR-002: pixi-viewport for Navigation**\n- **Pattern**: Google Maps-style pan/zoom with inertia\n- **Physics**: Native smooth zoom, pinch-to-zoom, momentum\n- **Alternative**: Custom physics (too expensive)\n\n**ADR-003: Virtual Scrolling (Math-based)**\n- **Rejected**: Ghost Div Pattern (DOM scrolling)\n- **Rejected**: react-window, react-virtualized\n- **Choice**: Physics-based virtual scroller for seamless fractal zoom\n\n**ADR-004: Structure of Arrays (SoA) with TypedArrays**\n- **Pattern**: Separate Float64Array, Uint16Array per property\n- **Benefit**: Zero GC during interaction, cache locality\n- **Tradeoff**: Verbose code, harder debugging\n- **Code example**: `startMs: Float64Array`, `aircraftIndex: Uint16Array`\n\n**ADR-005: @belt/tools State Machine (NOT React Hooks)**\n- **Rejected**: useState/useRef/useEffect (stale closures)\n- **Rejected**: Zustand (mutable state issues)\n- **Choice**: @belt/tools with `useSyncExternalStore`\n- **Benefit**: Synchronous state access, works in animation loops\n\n**ADR-006: Vite SPA** (NOT Next.js)\n- **Rejected**: Astro, Next.js (no SSR needed)\n- **Choice**: Vite 6 for minimal, fast dev server\n\n### 3.2 Code Patterns (code-patterns.md - 60k lines of reference)\n\n**Working Pattern: External Store + useSyncExternalStore**\n\n```typescript\n// viewport.ts\nclass ViewportStore {\n  private state: ViewportState;\n  private listeners: Set<Listener>;\n  \n  getState = (): ViewportState => this.state;\n  subscribe = (listener: Listener) => { /* ... */ };\n  \n  pan = (dx: number, dy: number) => { /* update state */ };\n  zoomIn = () => { /* update state */ };\n}\n\nexport const viewportStore = new ViewportStore();\n\nexport function useViewport() {\n  const state = useSyncExternalStore(viewportStore.subscribe, viewportStore.getState);\n  return { state, actions: { pan, zoomIn, ... } };\n}\n```\n\n**Benefit**: State always synchronous, no stale closures, works in `useFrame` loops\n\n**Working Pattern: Structure of Arrays (SoA)**\n\n```typescript\n// mockData.ts\nexport interface TaskSoA {\n  count: number;\n  ids: string[];\n  trainIds: Uint16Array;      // Per-task train index\n  startDays: Uint32Array;     // Per-task start day\n  durations: Uint32Array;     // Per-task duration\n  types: Uint8Array;          // Per-task type (0=preventive, 1=corrective...)\n}\n\nexport function tasksToSoA(tasks: Task[]): TaskSoA {\n  const soa: TaskSoA = {\n    count: tasks.length,\n    ids: tasks.map(t => t.id),\n    trainIds: new Uint16Array(tasks.map(t => t.trainId)),\n    startDays: new Uint32Array(tasks.map(t => t.startDay)),\n    durations: new Uint32Array(tasks.map(t => t.duration)),\n    types: new Uint8Array(tasks.map(t => TASK_TYPE_INDEX[t.type])),\n  };\n  return soa;\n}\n```\n\n**Benefit**: Contiguous memory, fast iteration, minimal GC\n\n**Working Pattern: InstancedMesh for Rendering**\n\n```typescript\n// MesoLayer.tsx - Task blocks using InstancedMesh\nfunction TaskBlocks({ tasks, conflictIndices }: TaskBlocksProps) {\n  const meshRef = useRef<THREE.InstancedMesh>(null);\n  \n  const { matrices, colors } = useMemo(() => {\n    const matrices = new Float32Array(tasks.count * 16);\n    const colors = new Float32Array(tasks.count * 3);\n    \n    for (let i = 0; i < tasks.count; i++) {\n      // Position: x = startDay * DAY_WIDTH + width/2\n      const x = tasks.startDays[i]! * DAY_WIDTH + ...\n      // Color: conflict? red : task type color\n      const color = conflictIndices?.has(i) ? CONFLICT_COLOR : typeColors[...];\n      // Store in arrays\n      dummy.matrix.toArray(matrices, i * 16);\n      colors[i * 3] = color[0]; // R\n      colors[i * 3 + 1] = color[1]; // G\n      colors[i * 3 + 2] = color[2]; // B\n    }\n    return { matrices, colors };\n  }, [tasks, conflictIndices]);\n  \n  // Apply to mesh once mounted\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const mesh = meshRef.current;\n    for (let i = 0; i < tasks.count; i++) {\n      const matrix = new THREE.Matrix4();\n      matrix.fromArray(matrices, i * 16);\n      mesh.setMatrixAt(i, matrix);\n    }\n    mesh.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate = true;\n    mesh.instanceColor.array = colors;\n    mesh.instanceColor.needsUpdate = true;\n  }, [matrices, colors]);\n  \n  return <instancedMesh ref={meshRef} args={[geometry, material, tasks.count]} />;\n}\n```\n\n**Benefit**: 60k tasks rendered in single draw call; GPU-accelerated batching\n\n**Working Pattern: LOD Transitions with Smoothstep**\n\n```typescript\n// viewport.ts\nexport function getLODOpacity(zoom: number) {\n  const smoothstep = (edge0: number, edge1: number, x: number) => {\n    const t = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (x - edge0) / (edge1 - edge0)));\n    return t * t * (3 - 2 * t); // Hermite interpolation\n  };\n  \n  let macro = 0, meso = 0, micro = 0;\n  \n  if (zoom < 0.425) {\n    macro = 1;\n  } else if (zoom < 0.575) {\n    // Crossfade zone (15% hysteresis)\n    macro = 1 - smoothstep(0.425, 0.575, zoom);\n    meso = smoothstep(0.425, 0.575, zoom);\n  } else if (zoom < 1.7) {\n    meso = 1;\n  } else if (zoom < 2.3) {\n    // Crossfade zone\n    meso = 1 - smoothstep(1.7, 2.3, zoom);\n    micro = smoothstep(1.7, 2.3, zoom);\n  } else {\n    micro = 1;\n  }\n  \n  return { macro, meso, micro };\n}\n```\n\n**Benefit**: Smooth crossfades between LOD layers; Hermite easing for visual polish\n\n**Anti-Pattern: Avoid**\n- ❌ useState in animation loops (stale closures)\n- ❌ useEffect for pan/zoom (update lag)\n- ❌ Array of Objects (cache miss locality)\n- ❌ Zustand for high-frequency updates (batching issues)\n- ❌ DOM rendering for 10k+ items (reflow/repaint)\n\n---\n\n## 4. USEFUL CODE PATTERNS & IMPLEMENTATIONS\n\n### 4.1 Viewport/Camera Management\n\n**Pattern**: World <-> Screen coordinate conversion\n```typescript\nexport function worldToScreen(\n  worldX: number, worldY: number, viewport: ViewportState\n): { x: number; y: number } {\n  const x = (worldX - viewport.x) * viewport.zoom + viewport.width / 2;\n  const y = (worldY - viewport.y) * viewport.zoom + viewport.height / 2;\n  return { x, y };\n}\n```\n\n**Usage**: Positioning UI overlays on canvas content\n\n### 4.2 Conflict Detection Algorithm\n\n```typescript\n// conflictDetection.ts\nexport function detectConflicts(tasks: TaskSoA): Conflict[] {\n  const conflicts: Conflict[] = [];\n  \n  for (let i = 0; i < tasks.count; i++) {\n    for (let j = i + 1; j < tasks.count; j++) {\n      // Same train, overlapping time?\n      if (tasks.trainIds[i] === tasks.trainIds[j]) {\n        const taskAEnd = tasks.startDays[i] + tasks.durations[i];\n        const taskBEnd = tasks.startDays[j] + tasks.durations[j];\n        \n        if (!(taskAEnd <= tasks.startDays[j] || taskBEnd <= tasks.startDays[i])) {\n          conflicts.push({ taskA: i, taskB: j });\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return conflicts;\n}\n```\n\n### 4.3 Mock Data Generation (Deterministic)\n\n```typescript\n// mockData.ts\nfunction seededRandom(seed: number) {\n  return function () {\n    seed = (seed * 1103515245 + 12345) & 0x7fffffff;\n    return seed / 0x7fffffff;\n  };\n}\n\nexport function generateMockTasks(\n  trainCount: number = 128,\n  yearsSpan: number = 12,\n  tasksPerTrainPerYear: number = 4\n): Task[] {\n  const random = seededRandom(42); // Same seed = same data\n  // ... generate 128 * 12 * 4 ≈ 6k tasks\n}\n```\n\n**Benefit**: Reproducible test data across sessions\n\n### 4.4 Scenario Management (Sandbox Mode)\n\n```typescript\n// scenario.ts\ninterface ScenarioState {\n  mode: \"production\" | \"sandbox\";\n  modifications: Map<string, TaskModification>;\n  isDirty: boolean;\n}\n\nconst SCENARIO_COLORS = {\n  production: { label: \"Production\", accent: \"#0066cc\", bg: \"#0a1a3a\" },\n  sandbox: { label: \"Sandbox\", accent: \"#8b5cf6\", bg: \"#2d1b4e\" },\n};\n```\n\n**Pattern**: Track modifications separately, apply to canvas with conditional rendering\n\n### 4.5 PatternFly 6 Integration\n\n```typescript\n// App.tsx\nimport {\n  Masthead, MastheadBrand, Page, PageSection,\n  Toolbar, ToolbarGroup, ToolbarItem, Label, Button\n} from \"@patternfly/react-core\";\nimport { MinusIcon, PlusIcon } from \"@patternfly/react-icons\";\n\n// Consistent with design system tokens\n// Light/dark theme via data-theme attribute\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. REUSABLE PATTERNS FOR MAIN PROJECT\n\n### Immediately Reusable:\n\n1. **Viewport Store Pattern** (`stores/viewport.ts`)\n   - Camera position, zoom, bounds\n   - useSyncExternalStore pattern\n   - World ↔ screen coordinate conversion\n\n2. **Structure of Arrays (SoA)** (`utils/mockData.ts`)\n   - Per-instance color via InstancedBufferAttribute\n   - Per-instance transforms via matrix array\n\n4. **LOD System** (`viewport.ts`)\n   - Smooth crossfade transitions (Hermite smoothstep)\n   - Hysteresis zones to prevent flicker\n   - Three LOD levels: MACRO/MESO/MICRO\n\n5. **Conflict Detection** (`utils/conflictDetection.ts`)\n   - O(n²) scan for time-overlapping tasks on same train\n   - Efficient for 60k tasks in mock scenario\n\n6. **Design System** (`design-system.md`)\n   - Feature passing/failing status\n   - Multi-phase sprint organization\n\n### Strategic Insights:\n\n1. **Never trust DOM rendering** for 10k+ items (use WebGL/canvas)\n2. **External stores** (not hooks) for animation loop synchronization\n3. **Separate data structure** from rendering (SoA separate from Three.js)\n4. **Competitor analysis** reveals genuine user needs (Marey charts, resource histograms)\n5. **Domain unknowns** (statutory tasks, bay limits, disruption mode) require proactive research\n6. **3-layer sandwich** (engine/canvas/HUD) keeps concerns cleanly separated\n7. **LOD with hysteresis** prevents jarring transitions during continuous zoom\n\n---\n\n## CONCLUSION\n\nThe mms-last-attempt project represents a **mature reference implementation** with:\n\n- ✅ **Comprehensive UX research** (competitor analysis + 10 unknown unknowns)\n- ✅ **Production code patterns** (SoA, InstancedMesh, external stores)\n- ✅ **Complete design system** (colors, typography, spacing, animations)\n- ✅ **Proven tech stack** (React 19, PatternFly 6, R3F 9, @belt/tools)\n- ✅ **Harness methodology** (session continuity, feature tracking)\n- ✅ **Critical domain insights** (statutory tasks, depot bays, KPIs, disruption mode)\n\n**Reusable in main project:**\n- Viewport store and camera management\n- Structure of Arrays data pattern\n- InstancedMesh rendering optimization\n- LOD system with smoothstep transitions\n- Design system tokens and theming\n- Conflict detection algorithm\n- Harness state tracking approach"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: aa540ed (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)"}]}]},"uuid":"e5d34a66-59b8-4230-bfb0-97ca1b0f5592","timestamp":"2026-01-09T22:12:42.441Z","toolUseResult":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Thoroughly explore the reference project at /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms-last-attempt. Unlike the main project, this reference contains:\n\n1. **Fully-researched UX documentation** with competitor analysis and domain discoveries\n2. **Production-ready implementation patterns** (React 19 + PatternFly 6 + R3F 9)\n3. **Comprehensive design system** with theming, typography, and spacing\n4. **Critical domain unknowns** discovered through deep research\n5. **Working code patterns** demonstrating state management, rendering, and performance optimization\n\nThe project demonstrates a sophisticated harness-based development approach and represents 6+ months of refinement across multiple attempts.\n\n---\n\n## 1. OVERALL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE\n\n```\nmms-last-attempt/\n├── src/\n│   ├── App.tsx                       # Main app shell (PatternFly + R3F)\n│   ├── main.tsx                      # Entry point\n│   ├── index.css                     # Theme variables and globals\n│   ├── components/\n│   │   ├── sidebar/FleetSidebar.tsx  # 128-train list with filters\n│   │   ├── panels/TaskDetailPanel.tsx # Task selection drawer\n│   │   └── canvas/\n│   │       ├── CanvasContainer.tsx   # R3F canvas wrapper\n│   │       └── RenderIndicator.tsx   # DOM verification component\n│   ├── render/\n│   │   ├── MacroLayer.tsx            # Heatmap visualization (LOD)\n│   │   ├── MesoLayer.tsx             # Test environment\n├── _memory/\n│   ├── ux-competitor-analysis.md     # IBM Maximo, Railnova, SignatureRail\n│   ├── ux-specification.md           # 2191-line UX bible\n│   ├── design-system.md              # Colors, typography, spacing\n│   ├── architecture-decisions.md     # ADR-001 through ADR-006+\n│   ├── code-patterns.md              # Working + anti-patterns\n│   ├── navigation-flows.md           # User journeys\n│   ├── ux-charts.md                  # Chart type specifications\n│   ├── ux-screens.md                 # Screen definitions\n│   ├── ux-unknown-unknowns.md        # 10 critical discoveries\n│   └── chart-types.md                # Heatmap, Gantt, Marey\n├── CLAUDE.md                          # Bootstrap instructions\n├── __brainstorming.md                # Cascade framework rules\n├── progress.txt                       # Harness state log\n├── features.json                      # Feature tracking (passes: true/false)\n├── package.json                       # React 19, PatternFly 6, R3F 9\n└── node_modules/                     # Installed dependencies\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. UX RESEARCH DOCUMENTS & FINDINGS\n\n### 2.1 Competitor Analysis (ux-competitor-analysis.md - 318 lines)\n\n**Three main competitors analyzed:**\n\n#### IBM Maximo Scheduler\n- **Strength**: Drag-drop Gantt + resource histogram (gold standard)\n- **Weakness**: Struggles at 10k+ records; discrete day/week/month tabs\n- **Steal**: Drag-drop capability, resource loading visualization\n\n#### Railnova (Brussels-based)\n- **Strength**: Event state pipeline (unplanned → planned → overdue → closed)\n- **Strength**: Digital train logbook for drivers/technicians\n- **Strength**: Color-coded anomaly detection\n- **Steal**: Event state machine, anomaly indicators, real-time monitoring\n\n#### SignatureRail (TrainPlan since 1994)\n- **Strength**: Marey chart visualization (industry gold standard since 1878)\n- **Strength**: What-if scenario management\n- **Marey advantage**: Superior time/space visualization for train scheduling\n- **Steal**: Marey chart implementation, scenario comparison\n\n**Gestalt Principles Applied:**\n- Proximity: Group related filters near views\n- Similarity: Consistent colors for data types\n- Enclosure: Cards with clear borders\n- Connectedness: Dependency lines between tasks\n- Closure: Minimal axis lines\n- Continuity: Chronological sorting\n\n**Differentiators:**\n| Feature | Competitors | MMS |\n|---------|-------------|-----|\n| Zoom | Discrete tabs | Continuous semantic zoom |\n| Performance | Struggles @ 10k | 60fps @ 60k tasks |\n| Interface | Fragmented modules | Unified canvas |\n| Visualization | Gantt only | Gantt + Marey + Heatmap (LOD) |\n\n### 2.2 Unknown Unknowns (ux-unknown-unknowns.md - 387 lines)\n\n**10 Critical Domain Discoveries:**\n\n1. **Statutory Tasks** (Non-moveable)\n   - Government-mandated safety inspections cannot be rescheduled\n   - UX: Lock icon + striped pattern\n   - Add to Task type: `isStatutory: boolean`\n\n2. **Depot Bay Limits** (Physical constraint)\n   - Al Rashidiya: 4-6 bays (49 Kinki Sharyo trains)\n   - Jebel Ali: 6-8 bays (79 Alstom trains)\n   - Cannot schedule more trains than bays available\n   - Need: Bay conflict detection (different from time conflicts)\n\n3. **Dependency Bottleneck** (Cascading changes)\n   - Moving one task can affect 12+ dependent tasks\n   - Performance: Cannot recalculate on every drag\n   - Solution: Debounced impact calculation (300ms), progressive disclosure\n\n4. **24-Hour Operation Reality**\n   - Maintenance windows: 5-6 hours/night (12:00am - 5:30am)\n   - Some special events: 24hr operation\n   - Need: Maintenance window visualization on timeline\n\n5. **Disruption Mode** (Disaster recovery)\n   - Dubai flooding (Apr 2024) closed stations for 38 days\n   - Need: Emergency schedule activation, backlog tracking, recovery planning\n\n6. **Standardized KPIs** (EN 15341 standard)\n   - MTBF: Mean Time Between Failures (higher = better)\n   - MTTR: Mean Time To Repair (lower = better)\n   - PM Compliance: > 95%\n   - Schedule Compliance: > 90%\n   - Need: KPI dashboard with trends and drill-down\n\n7. **Approval Workflows** (Multi-level)\n   - Depot Manager approves weekly schedule changes\n   - Operations Director approves overhauls\n   - Finance approves resource changes\n   - Need: Role-based access, approval queue, audit trail\n\n8. **Spare Parts Availability** (#1 delay cause)\n   - #1 reason tasks don't happen: parts unavailable\n   - Need: Parts status indicator (Green/Yellow/Red by ETA)\n   - Integration point: SAP/Maximo for stock levels\n\n9. **Mobile for Technicians**\n   - Floor-level staff need tablet interface (Railnova Digital Train Logbook pattern)\n   - \"My tasks today\" view\n   - Offline capability (caching)\n   - Photo upload for issues\n\n10. **Historical Data for Predictions**\n    - Track actual vs estimated duration\n    - Build ML model for future predictions\n    - Show confidence intervals (\"±30min confidence\")\n    - Anomaly detection (\"Train X needs 3x normal maintenance\")\n\n**Priority Matrix:**\n- **Must Have (Phase 1)**: Statutory locks, Bay capacity, KPIs, Impact preview\n- **Should Have (Phase 1.5)**: Maintenance windows, Approval workflow, Disruption mode\n- **Nice to Have (Phase 2)**: Parts availability, Mobile view, AI predictions\n\n### 2.3 Design System (design-system.md - 560 lines)\n\n**Color Palette (Dark Theme Primary):**\n- `--mms-bg-app`: #1a1a2e (main background)\n- `--mms-bg-card`: #16213e (card/panel)\n- `--mms-text-primary`: #f9fafb (near white)\n- `--canvas-bg`: #0A0A0A (pure black - OLED power savings)\n- Maintenance types: PM1 (green), PM2 (blue), HM (amber), OVH (red)\n- Status: Available (green), In-service (blue), Maintenance (red)\n- Variance: Green (good), Yellow (warning), Red (danger)\n\n**Typography:**\n- Primary font: Inter\n- Monospace: JetBrains Mono (train IDs, timestamps)\n- Sizes: 10px (xs) → 36px (4xl)\n- Weights: 500 (medium), 600 (semibold)\n\n**Spacing (4px base unit):**\n- `--mms-sidebar-width`: 130px (collapsed), 280px (expanded)\n- `--mms-navbar-height`: 44px\n- `--px-per-train`: 24px\n- `--px-per-day`: 2px\n- `--task-card-height`: 36px\n\n**LOD System:**\n```\nZoom:  0.05  0.35  0.50         2.00  2.15  5.0\nMACRO  [=====|-----|             |     |    ]\nMESO         [-----|=============|-----|    ]\nMICRO        |     [-----|=============|====]\n```\n- MACRO: < 0.5 zoom (heatmap, 100% opacity)\n- MESO: 0.5-2.0 zoom (DNA/bars, 100% opacity)\n- MICRO: > 2.0 zoom (detailed cards, 100% opacity)\n- Crossfade zones: 15% hysteresis overlap\n\n**Three-Layer Sandwich Architecture:**\n```\nLayer 3: HUD Overlay (HTML/React) - pointer-events: none\n  └─ Navbar, Sidebar, Timeline Header, Minimap, Tooltips\nLayer 2: Canvas (WebGL/R3F)\n  └─ Task bars, dependency lines, heatmaps, LOD layers\nLayer 1: Engine\n  └─ Constraint processing, data calculations\n```\n\n---\n\n## 3. DESIGN DECISIONS & PATTERNS\n\n### 3.1 Architecture Decision Records (architecture-decisions.md)\n\n**ADR-001: PixiJS v8 for WebGL** (NOT in final!)\n- **Rejected**: DOM/SVG rendering inadequate for 500k+ data points\n- **Choice**: PixiJS v8 (most performant 2D WebGL engine)\n- **Note**: Later evolved to React Three Fiber in final implementation\n\n**ADR-002: pixi-viewport for Navigation**\n- **Pattern**: Google Maps-style pan/zoom with inertia\n- **Physics**: Native smooth zoom, pinch-to-zoom, momentum\n- **Alternative**: Custom physics (too expensive)\n\n**ADR-003: Virtual Scrolling (Math-based)**\n- **Rejected**: Ghost Div Pattern (DOM scrolling)\n- **Rejected**: react-window, react-virtualized\n- **Choice**: Physics-based virtual scroller for seamless fractal zoom\n\n**ADR-004: Structure of Arrays (SoA) with TypedArrays**\n- **Pattern**: Separate Float64Array, Uint16Array per property\n- **Benefit**: Zero GC during interaction, cache locality\n- **Tradeoff**: Verbose code, harder debugging\n- **Code example**: `startMs: Float64Array`, `aircraftIndex: Uint16Array`\n\n**ADR-005: @belt/tools State Machine (NOT React Hooks)**\n- **Rejected**: useState/useRef/useEffect (stale closures)\n- **Rejected**: Zustand (mutable state issues)\n- **Choice**: @belt/tools with `useSyncExternalStore`\n- **Benefit**: Synchronous state access, works in animation loops\n\n**ADR-006: Vite SPA** (NOT Next.js)\n- **Rejected**: Astro, Next.js (no SSR needed)\n- **Choice**: Vite 6 for minimal, fast dev server\n\n### 3.2 Code Patterns (code-patterns.md - 60k lines of reference)\n\n**Working Pattern: External Store + useSyncExternalStore**\n\n```typescript\n// viewport.ts\nclass ViewportStore {\n  private state: ViewportState;\n  private listeners: Set<Listener>;\n  \n  getState = (): ViewportState => this.state;\n  subscribe = (listener: Listener) => { /* ... */ };\n  \n  pan = (dx: number, dy: number) => { /* update state */ };\n  zoomIn = () => { /* update state */ };\n}\n\nexport const viewportStore = new ViewportStore();\n\nexport function useViewport() {\n  const state = useSyncExternalStore(viewportStore.subscribe, viewportStore.getState);\n  return { state, actions: { pan, zoomIn, ... } };\n}\n```\n\n**Benefit**: State always synchronous, no stale closures, works in `useFrame` loops\n\n**Working Pattern: Structure of Arrays (SoA)**\n\n```typescript\n// mockData.ts\nexport interface TaskSoA {\n  count: number;\n  ids: string[];\n  trainIds: Uint16Array;      // Per-task train index\n  startDays: Uint32Array;     // Per-task start day\n  durations: Uint32Array;     // Per-task duration\n  types: Uint8Array;          // Per-task type (0=preventive, 1=corrective...)\n}\n\nexport function tasksToSoA(tasks: Task[]): TaskSoA {\n  const soa: TaskSoA = {\n    count: tasks.length,\n    ids: tasks.map(t => t.id),\n    trainIds: new Uint16Array(tasks.map(t => t.trainId)),\n    startDays: new Uint32Array(tasks.map(t => t.startDay)),\n    durations: new Uint32Array(tasks.map(t => t.duration)),\n    types: new Uint8Array(tasks.map(t => TASK_TYPE_INDEX[t.type])),\n  };\n  return soa;\n}\n```\n\n**Benefit**: Contiguous memory, fast iteration, minimal GC\n\n**Working Pattern: InstancedMesh for Rendering**\n\n```typescript\n// MesoLayer.tsx - Task blocks using InstancedMesh\nfunction TaskBlocks({ tasks, conflictIndices }: TaskBlocksProps) {\n  const meshRef = useRef<THREE.InstancedMesh>(null);\n  \n  const { matrices, colors } = useMemo(() => {\n    const matrices = new Float32Array(tasks.count * 16);\n    const colors = new Float32Array(tasks.count * 3);\n    \n    for (let i = 0; i < tasks.count; i++) {\n      // Position: x = startDay * DAY_WIDTH + width/2\n      const x = tasks.startDays[i]! * DAY_WIDTH + ...\n      // Color: conflict? red : task type color\n      const color = conflictIndices?.has(i) ? CONFLICT_COLOR : typeColors[...];\n      // Store in arrays\n      dummy.matrix.toArray(matrices, i * 16);\n      colors[i * 3] = color[0]; // R\n      colors[i * 3 + 1] = color[1]; // G\n      colors[i * 3 + 2] = color[2]; // B\n    }\n    return { matrices, colors };\n  }, [tasks, conflictIndices]);\n  \n  // Apply to mesh once mounted\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const mesh = meshRef.current;\n    for (let i = 0; i < tasks.count; i++) {\n      const matrix = new THREE.Matrix4();\n      matrix.fromArray(matrices, i * 16);\n      mesh.setMatrixAt(i, matrix);\n    }\n    mesh.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate = true;\n    mesh.instanceColor.array = colors;\n    mesh.instanceColor.needsUpdate = true;\n  }, [matrices, colors]);\n  \n  return <instancedMesh ref={meshRef} args={[geometry, material, tasks.count]} />;\n}\n```\n\n**Benefit**: 60k tasks rendered in single draw call; GPU-accelerated batching\n\n**Working Pattern: LOD Transitions with Smoothstep**\n\n```typescript\n// viewport.ts\nexport function getLODOpacity(zoom: number) {\n  const smoothstep = (edge0: number, edge1: number, x: number) => {\n    const t = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (x - edge0) / (edge1 - edge0)));\n    return t * t * (3 - 2 * t); // Hermite interpolation\n  };\n  \n  let macro = 0, meso = 0, micro = 0;\n  \n  if (zoom < 0.425) {\n    macro = 1;\n  } else if (zoom < 0.575) {\n    // Crossfade zone (15% hysteresis)\n    macro = 1 - smoothstep(0.425, 0.575, zoom);\n    meso = smoothstep(0.425, 0.575, zoom);\n  } else if (zoom < 1.7) {\n    meso = 1;\n  } else if (zoom < 2.3) {\n    // Crossfade zone\n    meso = 1 - smoothstep(1.7, 2.3, zoom);\n    micro = smoothstep(1.7, 2.3, zoom);\n  } else {\n    micro = 1;\n  }\n  \n  return { macro, meso, micro };\n}\n```\n\n**Benefit**: Smooth crossfades between LOD layers; Hermite easing for visual polish\n\n**Anti-Pattern: Avoid**\n- ❌ useState in animation loops (stale closures)\n- ❌ useEffect for pan/zoom (update lag)\n- ❌ Array of Objects (cache miss locality)\n- ❌ Zustand for high-frequency updates (batching issues)\n- ❌ DOM rendering for 10k+ items (reflow/repaint)\n\n---\n\n## 4. USEFUL CODE PATTERNS & IMPLEMENTATIONS\n\n### 4.1 Viewport/Camera Management\n\n**Pattern**: World <-> Screen coordinate conversion\n```typescript\nexport function worldToScreen(\n  worldX: number, worldY: number, viewport: ViewportState\n): { x: number; y: number } {\n  const x = (worldX - viewport.x) * viewport.zoom + viewport.width / 2;\n  const y = (worldY - viewport.y) * viewport.zoom + viewport.height / 2;\n  return { x, y };\n}\n```\n\n**Usage**: Positioning UI overlays on canvas content\n\n### 4.2 Conflict Detection Algorithm\n\n```typescript\n// conflictDetection.ts\nexport function detectConflicts(tasks: TaskSoA): Conflict[] {\n  const conflicts: Conflict[] = [];\n  \n  for (let i = 0; i < tasks.count; i++) {\n    for (let j = i + 1; j < tasks.count; j++) {\n      // Same train, overlapping time?\n      if (tasks.trainIds[i] === tasks.trainIds[j]) {\n        const taskAEnd = tasks.startDays[i] + tasks.durations[i];\n        const taskBEnd = tasks.startDays[j] + tasks.durations[j];\n        \n        if (!(taskAEnd <= tasks.startDays[j] || taskBEnd <= tasks.startDays[i])) {\n          conflicts.push({ taskA: i, taskB: j });\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  return conflicts;\n}\n```\n\n### 4.3 Mock Data Generation (Deterministic)\n\n```typescript\n// mockData.ts\nfunction seededRandom(seed: number) {\n  return function () {\n    seed = (seed * 1103515245 + 12345) & 0x7fffffff;\n    return seed / 0x7fffffff;\n  };\n}\n\nexport function generateMockTasks(\n  trainCount: number = 128,\n  yearsSpan: number = 12,\n  tasksPerTrainPerYear: number = 4\n): Task[] {\n  const random = seededRandom(42); // Same seed = same data\n  // ... generate 128 * 12 * 4 ≈ 6k tasks\n}\n```\n\n**Benefit**: Reproducible test data across sessions\n\n### 4.4 Scenario Management (Sandbox Mode)\n\n```typescript\n// scenario.ts\ninterface ScenarioState {\n  mode: \"production\" | \"sandbox\";\n  modifications: Map<string, TaskModification>;\n  isDirty: boolean;\n}\n\nconst SCENARIO_COLORS = {\n  production: { label: \"Production\", accent: \"#0066cc\", bg: \"#0a1a3a\" },\n  sandbox: { label: \"Sandbox\", accent: \"#8b5cf6\", bg: \"#2d1b4e\" },\n};\n```\n\n**Pattern**: Track modifications separately, apply to canvas with conditional rendering\n\n### 4.5 PatternFly 6 Integration\n\n```typescript\n// App.tsx\nimport {\n  Masthead, MastheadBrand, Page, PageSection,\n  Toolbar, ToolbarGroup, ToolbarItem, Label, Button\n} from \"@patternfly/react-core\";\nimport { MinusIcon, PlusIcon } from \"@patternfly/react-icons\";\n\n// Consistent with design system tokens\n// Light/dark theme via data-theme attribute\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. REUSABLE PATTERNS FOR MAIN PROJECT\n\n### Immediately Reusable:\n\n1. **Viewport Store Pattern** (`stores/viewport.ts`)\n   - Camera position, zoom, bounds\n   - useSyncExternalStore pattern\n   - World ↔ screen coordinate conversion\n\n2. **Structure of Arrays (SoA)** (`utils/mockData.ts`)\n   - Per-instance color via InstancedBufferAttribute\n   - Per-instance transforms via matrix array\n\n4. **LOD System** (`viewport.ts`)\n   - Smooth crossfade transitions (Hermite smoothstep)\n   - Hysteresis zones to prevent flicker\n   - Three LOD levels: MACRO/MESO/MICRO\n\n5. **Conflict Detection** (`utils/conflictDetection.ts`)\n   - O(n²) scan for time-overlapping tasks on same train\n   - Efficient for 60k tasks in mock scenario\n\n6. **Design System** (`design-system.md`)\n   - CSS custom properties for theming\n   - Color palette with semantic tokens\n   - Spacing and typography scales\n\n7. **Harness System** (`progress.txt`, `features.json`)\n   - Session continuity tracking\n   - Feature passing/failing status\n   - Multi-phase sprint organization\n\n### Strategic Insights:\n\n1. **Never trust DOM rendering** for 10k+ items (use WebGL/canvas)\n2. **External stores** (not hooks) for animation loop synchronization\n3. **Separate data structure** from rendering (SoA separate from Three.js)\n4. **Competitor analysis** reveals genuine user needs (Marey charts, resource histograms)\n5. **Domain unknowns** (statutory tasks, bay limits, disruption mode) require proactive research\n6. **3-layer sandwich** (engine/canvas/HUD) keeps concerns cleanly separated\n7. **LOD with hysteresis** prevents jarring transitions during continuous zoom\n\n---\n\n## CONCLUSION\n\nThe mms-last-attempt project represents a **mature reference implementation** with:\n\n- ✅ **Comprehensive UX research** (competitor analysis + 10 unknown unknowns)\n- ✅ **Production code patterns** (SoA, InstancedMesh, external stores)\n- ✅ **Complete design system** (colors, typography, spacing, animations)\n- ✅ **Proven tech stack** (React 19, PatternFly 6, R3F 9, @belt/tools)\n- ✅ **Harness methodology** (session continuity, feature tracking)\n- ✅ **Critical domain insights** (statutory tasks, depot bays, KPIs, disruption mode)\n\n**Reusable in main project:**\n- Viewport store and camera management\n- Structure of Arrays data pattern\n- InstancedMesh rendering optimization\n- LOD system with smoothstep transitions\n- Design system tokens and theming\n- Conflict detection algorithm\n- Harness state tracking approach"}],"totalDurationMs":91061,"totalTokens":91612,"totalToolUseCount":22,"usage":{"input_tokens":7,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7699,"cache_read_input_tokens":76598,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":7699,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":7308,"service_tier":"standard"}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"145ac1a7-8f03-4ddb-aef0-8811499fc733"}

APPS/CANVAS - CURRENT STATE\n\n### 2.1 Core Architecture\n\n**Tech Stack:**\n- **React 18.3.1** + **Vite 5.x** (build/dev)\n- **React Three Fiber 8.17.10** + **Three.js 0.170.0** (WebGL)\n- **drei 9.114.3** (R3F helpers: Html, Line, etc.)\n- **BlueprintJS 6.1.0** (UI components: Navbar, Card, Button)\n- **@zazcart/tools 0.1.0** (State machine library)\n- **rbush 4.0.1** (R-tree spatial indexing)\n- **D3 modules** (d3-array, d3-scale, d3-time, d3-scale-chromatic, d3-zoom, etc.)\n\n### 2.2 Application Structure\n\n**Entry Point**: `App.tsx` (395 lines)\n- Renders navbar with zoom controls\n- Sidebar with 50 trains (clickable navigation)\n- TimelineHeader (date navigation)\n- InfiniteCanvas (R3F rendering)\n- Minimap (bird's eye view)\n- TaskTooltip (hover info)\n- TaskDetailsPanel (selection)\n- PerformancePanel (FPS stats)\n\n**Canvas Rendering**: `InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (2106 lines - VERY LARGE)\n- OrthographicCamera setup\n- Pan/zoom interaction handling\n- LOD (Level of Detail) system with smooth transitions\n- Three layers: MacroLayer, MesoLayer, MicroLayer\n- Viewport culling with RBush spatial index\n- Momentum/inertial panning\n- Animation state machine\n\n**Layers**:\n- **MacroLayer** (Heatmap): Visible at zoom < 0.5. Uses InstancedMesh + d3-scale-chromatic for yellow→orange→red gradient\n- **MesoLayer** (Task Blocks): Visible at zoom 0.5-2.0. InstancedMesh for all task blocks with conflict indicators\n- **MicroLayer** (Detailed Cards): Visible at zoom > 2.0. HTML overlays with task details, grid lines, labels\n\n**State Management**: `state/`\n- `canvas-machine.ts`: Creates state machine with two slices (viewport, interaction)\n- `viewport.ts`: Camera position, momentum, animation, canvas size\n- `interaction.ts`: Hover/selection/focus state for tasks\n- Uses `@zazcart/tools` with `metadata: false` for 60fps optimization\n\n### 2.3 Data & Types\n\n**Mock Data Generator** (`data/generator.ts`):\n- 50 trains (30 ALSTOM, 20 KINKI Sharyo)\n- 10 years of tasks per train\n- ~8-12 tasks per train per year\n- ~5% have conflict flags\n- Generated on-demand (no persistence)\n\n**Domain Types** (`data/types.ts`):\n- `Train`: id, name, assetType\n- `Task`: id, trainId, startDate, endDate, type, hasConflict, predecessorId\n- `TaskRender`: Computed positions (trainIndex, startDay, duration, color)\n- `WorldBounds`: Navigation limits\n- `ViewConfig`: Pixel scales (pxPerDay=10, pxPerTrain=24)\n\n---\n\n## 3. STATE MANAGEMENT IMPLEMENTATION (@zazcart/tools)\n\n### 3.1 Usage Pattern\n\n```typescript\n// canvas-machine.ts\nexport const canvasMachine = createMachine({\n  id: 'canvas',\n  slices: {\n    viewport: viewportSlice,      // Camera, momentum, animation\n    interaction: interactionSlice, // Hover, selection, focus\n  },\n  metadata: false, // ⚠️ CRITICAL: Disables metadata tracking for 60fps\n});\n\n// Component access\nconst useCanvas = canvasMachine.useMachine;\nconst useCanvasSelector = canvasMachine.useSelector;\nconst canvasStore = { getState, dispatch, subscribe };\n```\n\n### 3.2 Viewport Slice (viewport.ts)\n\n**State**:\n- `camera`: { x, y } - world coordinates\n- `canvasSize`: { width, height }\n- `isInitialized`: boolean\n- `momentum`: { velocityX, velocityY, isActive }\n- `animation`: { isAnimating, startTime, duration, startX/Y/Zoom, targetX/Y/Zoom }\n- `isPanning`: boolean\n- Pan start/camera positions\n\n**Reducers**:\n- `setCamera(pos)`, `setCameraX(x)`, `setCameraY(y)`\n- `setCanvasSize(size)`\n- `initialize({ camera, canvasSize })`\n- `startMomentum`, `updateMomentum`, `stopMomentum`\n- `startAnimation`, `updateAnimation`, `stopAnimation`\n- `startPan`, `updatePan`, `endPan`\n- `reset()`\n\n### 3.3 Interaction Slice (interaction.ts)\n\n**State**:\n- `hoveredTaskId`: string | null (F018)\n- `mouseWorldPos`: { x, y } | null\n- `selectedTaskId`: string | null (F019)\n- `focusedTaskId`: string | null (F021 - keyboard navigation)\n- `isHoverable`: boolean\n\n**Reducers**:\n- `setHoveredTask(id)`, `clearHover()`\n- `setSelectedTask(id)`, `clearSelection()`\n- `setFocusedTask(id)`, `clearFocus()`\n- `setMouseWorldPos(pos)`\n- `reset()`\n\n---\n\n## 4. CANVAS/R3F IMPLEMENTATION\n\n### 4.1 LOD System\n\n**Three-Layer Architecture** with smooth blending:\n\n| LOD | Zoom | Render Method | Visibility |\n|-----|------|---------------|-----------|\n| **MACRO** | < 0.5x | Heatmap (InstancedMesh) | Strategic overview - monthly cells |\n| **MESO** | 0.5-2.0x | Task blocks (InstancedMesh) | Default view - DNA pattern |\n| **MICRO** | > 2.0x | Detail cards (Html) | Detailed inspection - labels, grid |\n\n**Blend Zones** (±0.15 around thresholds):\n- MACRO → MESO at 0.35-0.65 zoom\n- MESO → MICRO at 1.85-2.15 zoom\n- Smooth opacity transitions between layers\n\n**Implementation**:\n- `calculateLayerOpacities(zoom)` → { macro, meso, micro } opacities\n- `LODLayers` component renders all visible layers with interpolated opacity\n- Each layer controls its own visibility/culling\n\n### 4.2 Performance Optimizations\n\n1. **InstancedMesh Pre-allocation** (PERF-001):\n   - Single draw call per layer (instead of 1000s)\n   - Pre-allocate 100k instances max\n   - Use `mesh.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate` for dynamic updates\n\n2. **Viewport Culling** (PERF-002):\n   - RBushSpatialIndex for O(log n) queries\n   - `calculateViewportBounds()` expands render viewport slightly\n   - MicroLayer: `filterVisibleTasks()` reduces HTML elements\n   - Limit to 50 HTML labels in MicroLayer\n\n3. **Object Pooling**:\n   - Reuse Object3D temp objects in loops\n   - Avoid allocations in animation loops\n\n4. **GC Avoidance**:\n   - `@zazcart/tools` configured with `metadata: false`\n   - Prevents object allocations on every dispatch\n   - Direct state mutations (Immer-based)\n\n### 4.3 Interaction Handling\n\n**Pan**:\n- Mouse drag updates camera position\n- Bounds-checked to world limits\n- Momentum physics option (currently disabled - BUG-008)\n\n**Zoom**:\n- Scroll wheel (Google Maps-style)\n- Trackpad pinch support (with workarounds for BUG-009)\n- Zoom centered on cursor position\n- Smooth animation (250ms) vs direct application (trackpad)\n\n**Gestures**:\n- Keyboard: Arrow keys for pan, +/- for zoom (F027, F028)\n- Click: Select tasks (F019)\n- Hover: Highlight tasks + tooltip (F018, F020)\n- Keyboard focus: Navigate tasks (F021)\n\n---\n\n## 5. BLUEPRINTJS USAGE PATTERNS\n\n### 5.1 Components Used\n\n```tsx\n// Navbar with groups\n<Navbar fixedToTop={false}>\n  <NavbarGroup align={Alignment.LEFT}>\n    <NavbarHeading>MMS Canvas</NavbarHeading>\n    <NavbarDivider />\n    <Tag intent={Intent.PRIMARY}>MESO</Tag>\n  </NavbarGroup>\n  <NavbarGroup align={Alignment.RIGHT}>\n    <ButtonGroup minimal>\n      <Button icon=\"minus\" onClick={handleZoomOut} />\n      <Button icon=\"plus\" onClick={handleZoomIn} />\n    </ButtonGroup>\n  </NavbarGroup>\n</Navbar>\n\n// Card for sidebar\n<Card elevation={1} style={{ /* layout */ }}>\n  <div>Header</div>\n  <div>Scrollable content</div>\n</Card>\n\n// Toast notifications\nOverlayToaster.createAsync({\n  position: Position.TOP,\n  className: 'instructions-toaster',\n}).then(toaster => {\n  toaster.show({\n    message: 'Scroll to zoom • Drag to pan',\n    intent: Intent.PRIMARY,\n    timeout: 5000,\n  });\n});\n```\n\n### 5.2 CSS Classes\n\n- Blueprint uses `bp6-*` prefix (v6.x)\n- Dark mode: `bp6-dark` class\n- All styling via Blueprint CSS + inline styles\n- No custom CSS framework (just utilities)\n\n---\n\n## 6. OVERALL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE\n\n```\nsystem/                           # Monorepo root\n├── apps/\n│   └── canvas/                   # MAIN PRODUCTION APP (Vite + React 18)\n│       ├── src/\n│       │   ├── main.tsx\n│       │   ├── App.tsx           # Root component (2106 lines - LARGE)\n│       │   ├── canvas/           # R3F rendering\n│       │   │   ├── InfiniteCanvas.tsx  (2106 lines - LARGE)\n│       │   │   └── layers/\n│       │   │       ├── MacroLayer.tsx   (Heatmap visualization)\n│       │   │       ├── MesoLayer.tsx    (Task blocks, InstancedMesh)\n│       │   │       └── MicroLayer.tsx   (Detailed cards)\n│       │   ├── components/       (BlueprintJS components)\n│       │   │   ├── Minimap.tsx\n│       │   │   ├── PerfMonitor.tsx\n│       │   │   ├── StatsMonitor.tsx\n│       │   │   ├── TaskDetailsPanel.tsx\n│       │   │   ├── TaskTooltip.tsx\n│       │   │   └── TimelineHeader.tsx\n│       │   ├── data/\n│       │   │   ├── types.ts      (Domain + render types)\n│       │   │   └── generator.ts  (Mock data: 50 trains × 10 years)\n│       │   ├── state/            (@zazcart/tools state machine)\n│       │   │   ├── canvas-machine.ts\n│       │   │   ├── viewport.ts   (Camera, momentum, animation)\n│       │   │   └── interaction.ts (Hover, selection, focus)\n│       │   ├── spatial/          (Viewport culling - R-tree based)\n│       │   │   ├── RBushSpatialIndex.ts\n│       │   │   ├── useSpatialIndex.ts\n│       │   │   └── types.ts\n│       │   └── entity-store/     (DEAD CODE - Not used in app)\n│       │       ├── EntityStore.ts      (TypedArray-based entity storage)\n│       │       ├── useEntityStore.ts   (Unused hook)\n│       │       └── types.ts\n│       ├── package.json\n│       └── vite.config.ts\n│\n├── docs/                         (18 markdown files, ~16KB total)\n│   ├── SPECIFICATION.md          (2191 lines - PRIMARY SPEC)\n│   ├── MMS_COMPLETE_SPECIFICATION.md (8922 lines - EXTENDED/DETAILED)\n│   ├── DOMAIN.md\n│   ├── UX_SPECIFICATION.md\n│   ├── PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md\n│   ├── QA_PROTOCOL.md\n│   ├── UX_RESEARCH.md\n│   ├── VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md\n│   ├── RESEARCH.md\n│   └── deck-research/\n│\n├── design/                       (Reference POC + screenshots)\n│   ├── README.md\n│   ├── reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html\n│   ├── pins/scheduler_01.jpeg\n│   └── screen-*.png (7 screenshots)\n│\n├── .claude/                      (Harness state files)\n│   └── hooks/\n│\n├── genesis/                      (Session history - 6.9MB)\n│   └── attempt6202/conversations.jsonl\n│\n├── CLAUDE.md                     (V1.0 Genesis quick start)\n├── CLAUDE_NOTES.md               (Technology notes & gotchas)\n├── README.md                     (Project README)\n├── progress.txt                  (82KB session log)\n├── features.json                 (Feature status & priorities)\n├── package.json                  (Monorepo root)\n├── tsconfig.base.json\n└── turbo.json\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. APPS/CANVAS - CURRENT STATE\n\n### 2.1 Core Architecture\n\n**Tech Stack:**\n- **React 18.3.1** + **Vite 5.x** (build/dev)\n- **React Three Fiber 8.17.10** + **Three.js 0.170.0** (WebGL)\n- **drei 9.114.3** (R3F helpers: Html, Line, etc.)\n- **BlueprintJS 6.1.0** (UI components: Navbar, Card, Button)\n- **@zazcart/tools 0.1.0** (State machine library)\n- **rbush 4.0.1** (R-tree spatial indexing)\n- **D3 modules** (d3-array, d3-scale, d3-time, d3-scale-chromatic, d3-zoom, etc.)\n\n### 2.2 Application Structure\n\n**Entry Point**: `App.tsx` (395 lines)\n- Renders navbar with zoom controls\n- Sidebar with 50 trains (clickable navigation)\n- TimelineHeader (date navigation)\n- InfiniteCanvas (R3F rendering)\n- Minimap (bird's eye view)\n- TaskTooltip (hover info)\n- TaskDetailsPanel (selection)\n- PerformancePanel (FPS stats)\n\n**Canvas Rendering**: `InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (2106 lines - VERY LARGE)\n- OrthographicCamera setup\n- Pan/zoom interaction handling\n- LOD (Level of Detail) system with smooth transitions\n- Three layers: MacroLayer, MesoLayer, MicroLayer\n- Viewport culling with RBush spatial index\n- Momentum/inertial panning\n- Animation state machine\n\n**Layers**:\n- **MacroLayer** (Heatmap): Visible at zoom < 0.5. Uses InstancedMesh + d3-scale-chromatic for yellow→orange→red gradient\n- **MesoLayer** (Task Blocks): Visible at zoom 0.5-2.0. InstancedMesh for all task blocks with conflict indicators\n- **MicroLayer** (Detailed Cards): Visible at zoom > 2.0. HTML overlays with task details, grid lines, labels\n\n**State Management**: `state/`\n- `canvas-machine.ts`: Creates state machine with two slices (viewport, interaction)\n- `viewport.ts`: Camera position, momentum, animation, canvas size\n- `interaction.ts`: Hover/selection/focus state for tasks\n- Uses `@zazcart/tools` with `metadata: false` for 60fps optimization\n\n### 2.3 Data & Types\n\n**Mock Data Generator** (`data/generator.ts`):\n- 50 trains (30 ALSTOM, 20 KINKI Sharyo)\n- 10 years of tasks per train\n- ~8-12 tasks per train per year\n- ~5% have conflict flags\n- Generated on-demand (no persistence)\n\n**Domain Types** (`data/types.ts`):\n- `Train`: id, name, assetType\n- `Task`: id, trainId, startDate, endDate, type, hasConflict, predecessorId\n- `TaskRender`: Computed positions (trainIndex, startDay, duration, color)\n- `WorldBounds`: Navigation limits\n- `ViewConfig`: Pixel scales (pxPerDay=10, pxPerTrain=24)\n\n---\n\n## 3. STATE MANAGEMENT IMPLEMENTATION (@zazcart/tools)\n\n### 3.1 Usage Pattern\n\n```typescript\n// canvas-machine.ts\nexport const canvasMachine = createMachine({\n  id: 'canvas',\n  slices: {\n    viewport: viewportSlice,      // Camera, momentum, animation\n    interaction: interactionSlice, // Hover, selection, focus\n  },\n  metadata: false, // ⚠️ CRITICAL: Disables metadata tracking for 60fps\n});\n\n// Component access\nconst useCanvas = canvasMachine.useMachine;\nconst useCanvasSelector = canvasMachine.useSelector;\nconst canvasStore = { getState, dispatch, subscribe };\n```\n\n### 3.2 Viewport Slice (viewport.ts)\n\n**State**:\n- `camera`: { x, y } - world coordinates\n- `canvasSize`: { width, height }\n- `isInitialized`: boolean\n- `momentum`: { velocityX, velocityY, isActive }\n- `animation`: { isAnimating, startTime, duration, startX/Y/Zoom, targetX/Y/Zoom }\n- `isPanning`: boolean\n- Pan start/camera positions\n\n**Reducers**:\n- `setCamera(pos)`, `setCameraX(x)`, `setCameraY(y)`\n- `setCanvasSize(size)`\n- `initialize({ camera, canvasSize })`\n- `startMomentum`, `updateMomentum`, `stopMomentum`\n- `startAnimation`, `updateAnimation`, `stopAnimation`\n- `startPan`, `updatePan`, `endPan`\n- `reset()`\n\n### 3.3 Interaction Slice (interaction.ts)\n\n**State**:\n- `hoveredTaskId`: string | null (F018)\n- `mouseWorldPos`: { x, y } | null\n- `selectedTaskId`: string | null (F019)\n- `focusedTaskId`: string | null (F021 - keyboard navigation)\n- `isHoverable`: boolean\n\n**Reducers**:\n- `setHoveredTask(id)`, `clearHover()`\n- `setSelectedTask(id)`, `clearSelection()`\n- `setFocusedTask(id)`, `clearFocus()`\n- `setMouseWorldPos(pos)`\n- `reset()`\n\n---\n\n## 4. CANVAS/R3F IMPLEMENTATION\n\n### 4.1 LOD System\n\n**Three-Layer Architecture** with smooth blending:\n\n| LOD | Zoom | Render Method | Visibility |\n|-----|------|---------------|-----------|\n| **MACRO** | < 0.5x | Heatmap (InstancedMesh) | Strategic overview - monthly cells |\n| **MESO** | 0.5-2.0x | Task blocks (InstancedMesh) | Default view - DNA pattern |\n| **MICRO** | > 2.0x | Detail cards (Html) | Detailed inspection - labels, grid |\n\n**Blend Zones** (±0.15 around thresholds):\n- MACRO → MESO at 0.35-0.65 zoom\n- MESO → MICRO at 1.85-2.15 zoom\n- Smooth opacity transitions between layers\n\n**Implementation**:\n- `calculateLayerOpacities(zoom)` → { macro, meso, micro } opacities\n- `LODLayers` component renders all visible layers with interpolated opacity\n- Each layer controls its own visibility/culling\n\n### 4.2 Performance Optimizations\n\n1. **InstancedMesh Pre-allocation** (PERF-001):\n   - Single draw call per layer (instead of 1000s)\n   - Pre-allocate 100k instances max\n   - Use `mesh.instanceMatrix.needsUpdate` for dynamic updates\n\n2. **Viewport Culling** (PERF-002):\n   - RBushSpatialIndex for O(log n) queries\n   - `calculateViewportBounds()` expands render viewport slightly\n   - MicroLayer: `filterVisibleTasks()` reduces HTML elements\n   - Limit to 50 HTML labels in MicroLayer\n\n3. **Object Pooling**:\n   - Reuse Object3D temp objects in loops\n   - Avoid allocations in animation loops\n\n4. **GC Avoidance**:\n   - `@zazcart/tools` configured with `metadata: false`\n   - Prevents object allocations on every dispatch\n   - Direct state mutations (Immer-based)\n\n### 4.3 Interaction Handling\n\n**Pan**:\n- Mouse drag updates camera position\n- Bounds-checked to world limits\n- Momentum physics option (currently disabled - BUG-008)\n\n**Zoom**:\n- Scroll wheel (Google Maps-style)\n- Trackpad pinch support (with workarounds for BUG-009)\n- Zoom centered on cursor position\n- Smooth animation (250ms) vs direct application (trackpad)\n\n**Gestures**:\n- Keyboard: Arrow keys for pan, +/- for zoom (F027, F028)\n- Click: Select tasks (F019)\n- Hover: Highlight tasks + tooltip (F018, F020)\n- Keyboard focus: Navigate tasks (F021)\n\n---\n\n## 5. BLUEPRINTJS USAGE PATTERNS\n\n### 5.1 Components Used\n\n```tsx\n// Navbar with groups\n<Navbar fixedToTop={false}>\n  <NavbarGroup align={Alignment.LEFT}>\n    <NavbarHeading>MMS Canvas</NavbarHeading>\n    <NavbarDivider />\n    <Tag intent={Intent.PRIMARY}>MESO</Tag>\n  </NavbarGroup>\n  <NavbarGroup align={Alignment.RIGHT}>\n    <ButtonGroup minimal>\n      <Button icon=\"minus\" onClick={handleZoomOut} />\n      <Button icon=\"plus\" onClick={handleZoomIn} />\n    </ButtonGroup>\n  </NavbarGroup>\n</Navbar>\n\n// Card for sidebar\n<Card elevation={1} style={{ /* layout */ }}>\n  <div>Header</div>\n  <div>Scrollable content</div>\n</Card>\n\n// Toast notifications\nOverlayToaster.createAsync({\n  position: Position.TOP,\n  className: 'instructions-toaster',\n}).then(toaster => {\n  toaster.show({\n    message: 'Scroll to zoom • Drag to pan',\n    intent: Intent.PRIMARY,\n    timeout: 5000,\n  });\n});\n```\n\n### 5.2 CSS Classes\n\n- Blueprint uses `bp6-*` prefix (v6.x)\n- Dark mode: `bp6-dark` class\n- All styling via Blueprint CSS + inline styles\n- No custom CSS framework (just utilities)\n\n---\n\n## 6. SPATIAL INDEX & VIEWPORT CULLING\n\n**Implementation**: RBushSpatialIndex (useSpatialIndex hook)\n\n```typescript\n// InfiniteCanvas.tsx\nconst spatialIndex = useSpatialIndex(tasks);\nconst viewportBounds = calculateViewportBounds(/* ... */);\nconst visibleTaskIds = useVisibleTaskIds(spatialIndex, viewportBounds);\n\n// Passed to layers\n<MesoLayer \n  tasks={tasks}\n  viewportBounds={viewportBounds}\n  spatialIndex={spatialIndex}\n/>\n```\n\n**Performance**:\n- RBush O(log n) queries vs O(n) naive filtering\n- Expected: ~0.5ms for 1000 visible tasks\n- Actual: 120fps maintained\n- Visible instances culled from 60k to ~500 per frame\n\n---\n\n## 16. DATA FLOW SUMMARY\n\n```\nMock Data Generator\n    ↓\n    └→ Train[], Task[] (domain types)\n        ↓\n        └→ getRenderData() → TaskRender[] (computed positions)\n            ↓\n            ├→ RBush Spatial Index (world bounds)\n            │   ↓\n            │   Viewport Culling (visible tasks only)\n            │\n            ├→ MacroLayer (heatmap, if zoom < 0.5)\n            ├→ MesoLayer (blocks, if zoom 0.5-2.0)\n            └→ MicroLayer (details, if zoom > 2.0)\n                ↓\n                All layers pass state → @zazcart/tools (hover, select, focus)\n                ↓\n                Components re-render on state changes\n```\n\n---\n\n## 17. \"style={{.*color:\"\n  \"style={{.*background:\"\n  \"import.*from.*@patternfly\"\n  \"import.*from.*@mui\"\n  \"import.*from.*antd\"\n)\n\nfor pattern in \"${BLOCKED_PATTERNS[@]}\"; do\n  if grep \"style={{.*color:\"\n  \"style={{.*background:\"\n  \"import.*from.*@patternfly\"\n  \"import.*from.*@mui\"\n  \"import.*from.*antd\"\n)\n\nfor pattern in \"${BLOCKED_PATTERNS[@]}\"; do\n  if grep Task types: PREVENT (green), CORRECT (orange), OVERHAUL (red)\n\n### Component Mapping\n| Need | BlueprintJS Component |\n|------|----------------------|\n| Navbar | `<Navbar>` + `<NavbarGroup>` |\n| / `<HTMLSelect>` |\n\n### Spacing Scale (4px base)\n- xs: 4px, sm: 8px, md: 12px, lg: 16px, xl: 24px\n\n### Typography\n- Use Blueprint's built-in typography classes\n- Monospace for train IDs: `bp6-monospace-text`\n```\n\n---\n\n## Phase 5: Archive Stale Documentation\n\n### 5.1 Move to Archive\n```\ndocs/RESEARCH.md → docs/archive/RESEARCH.md\ndocs/PARALLEL_EXECUTION.md → IDENTIFIED","-","-**Objective:**","-Deep research and planning for viewport culling optimization + Investigation of trackpad zoom performance issue on MacBook.","-","-**Research Conducted:**","-- Web searches: Three.js frustum culling, R3F viewport culling, RBush spatial indexing, deck.gl massive rendering, Figma WebGL architecture","-- Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages)","-- Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant)","-- Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation)","-","-**Key Findings:**","-","-1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport","-   - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame","-   - No built-in per-instance frustum culling","-","-2. **RBush Spatial Index:**","-   - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time)","-   - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms","-   - Perfect for viewport culling queries","-","-3. **Culling Strategy:**","-   - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer","-   - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered)","-   - No new allocations during pan/zoom","-","-4. **Alternative Libraries:**","-   - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling","-   - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting","-   - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data","-","-**Documents Created:**","-- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Comprehensive research consolidating all findings","-","-**Task Created: PERF-002**","-","-Added to features.json with 4 implementation phases:","-","-1. **Phase 1 - Spatial Index:** RBush R-tree for task/entity lookup","-2. **Phase 2 - Culled InstancedMesh:** Instance reordering + count manipulation","-3. **Phase 3 - Layer Integration:** MesoLayer, MacroLayer viewport-aware","-4. **Phase 4 - Validation:** Performance testing and profiling","-","-**Dependency Update:**","-- STATE-ARCH-001 now `blockedBy: \"PERF-002\"`","-- PERF-002 is incremental improvement that can be done BEFORE full architecture migration","-- Establishes viewport culling foundation that STATE-ARCH-001 will build upon","-","-**Performance Targets:**","-- Viewport query: <5ms for 1000 results","-- Instance update: <1ms for 1000 visible","-- FPS: 60fps constant during pan/zoom","-- GPU instances: Only visible count (not all 60k)","-","-**BUG-009 Investigation (Added):**","-- User reported: Trackpad zoom has lag/stutter, but minimap navigation is smooth","-- Diagnosis: This is INPUT HANDLING issue, not rendering (minimap proves rendering is fine)","-- Root cause candidates:","-  - ZOOM_ANIMATION_DURATION=250ms creates queued animations","-  - ZOOM_ACCUMULATION_TIMEOUT=50ms delays execution","-  - animateZoomTo() starts new RAF instead of updating target","-- Solution options:","-  - Option A: Remove animation for trackpad (direct zoom like minimap)","-  - Option B: Update target during animation","-  - Option C: Use d3-zoom or @use-gesture/react (market solutions)","-","-**Files Changed:**","-- `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` (NEW - comprehensive research)","-- `features.json`:","-  - Added PERF-002 with 5 phases (phase0 = BUG-009 fix first)","-  - Added BUG-009 with detailed investigation","-  - Updated STATE-ARCH-001 blockedBy: PERF-002","-- `claude-progress.txt` (this file)","-","-**Alignment Fix (Added):**","-- PERF-002.Phase1 was duplicating STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 (both had RBush)","-- Fix: PERF-002.Phase1 now defines ISpatialIndex INTERFACE only + placeholder","-- STATE-ARCH-001.Phase2 implements full RBush with TypedArrays","-- No more duplicate work - tasks are now complementary","-","-**Status:** Planning complete. Tasks aligned. 3 groups):","-├── Group A: F023 (Dependency lines) → MicroLayer.tsx [LOW risk]","-├── Group B: F123, F025 (Minimap) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk, sequential]","-└── Group C: F016, F017 (Scroll modifiers) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]","-","-WAVE 2 (After Wave 1 merge - 2 groups):","-├── Group D: F027, F028 (Keyboard shortcuts) → InfiniteCanvas.tsx [HIGH risk, sequential]","-└── Group E: F026 (Task details panel) → App.tsx [MEDIUM risk]","-","-WAVE 3 (After Wave 2 merge - Added `.trees/` for worktree directories","-- `features.json` - Added `parallelExecution` section with waves configuration","-","-**Key Features of Parallel Runner:**","-1. **Git worktree management** - Creates isolated working directories","-2. **Terminal session spawning** - Opens separate Claude sessions per feature","-3. **Isolation prompts** - Each session restricted to its feature scope","-4. **Wave dependencies** - Prevents starting Wave N before Wave N-1 completes","-5. **Merge helper** - Automates feature branch merging","-6. **Status display** - Shows progress across all waves/groups","-7. **macOS notifications** - Alerts for session events","-8. **Cleanup command** - Removes all worktrees","-","-**Speedup Calculation:**","-- Sequential: 10 features = 10 sessions","-- Parallel: 4 waves (2+2+1+1) = ~4 \"effective sessions\"","-- Estimated speedup: **2.5x**","-","-**Usage:**","-```bash","-# Show status","-bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts status","-","-# Execute Wave 1","-bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts wave 1","-","-# Merge completed feature","-bun scripts/parallel-runner.ts merge F023","-```","-","----","-","-## Session: 2025-12-17 (F020 Tooltip) - COMPLETE","-","-**Problem:**","-Canvas felt elastic or jumped when starting a new drag during/after momentum animation.","-","-**Root Cause Analysis:**","-React state updates are batched/async. When user starts a new drag while momentum is running:","-1. `handlePointerDown` captures `cameraPos` from React state","-2. But React state hasn't been updated yet with latest momentum position","-3. New drag starts from stale position → canvas \"jumps\" back","-","-**Solution Implemented:**","-1. Added `cameraPosRef` to track camera position synchronously via useRef","-2. Created `updateCameraPos()` helper that updates BOTH ref and state","-3. Updated all `setCameraPos()` calls to use `updateCameraPos()`:","-   - `handleCameraInitialized`","-   - `animateZoomTo` (zoom animation loop)","-   - `startMomentum` (momentum animation loop)","-   - `handlePinchZoom`","-   - `handlePointerMove` (pan movement)","-   - `handleDoubleClick` (double-click zoom animation)","-4. Changed `handlePointerDown` to read from `cameraPosRef.current` instead of `cameraPos` state","-","-**Files Changed:**","-- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (cameraPosRef, updateCameraPos helper)","-- `features.json` (F122 passes: true)","-","-**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**","-- ✅ Single drag pans correctly","-- ✅ Drag during momentum - no jump/elastic effect","-- ✅ Rapid drag-release-drag sequences (5 iterations) - smooth panning","-- ✅ No console errors","-","-### Harness Updates","-","-**F117 Archived:**","-- \"Full visual parity with POC reference\" no longer relevant","-- Production app has surpassed POC in functionality and UX","-- Moved to archive.deferredFeatures","-","-**F124 Added (Feature Request):**","-- Sidebar and Timeline interactive implemented in F119 (smooth zoom) session","-- `handleDoubleClick` function in InfiniteCanvas.tsx (lines 1009-1074)","-- Zooms 2x toward click point with 350ms cubicOut animation","-- Respects (clamped to MAX_ZOOM)","-- ✅ Double-click at 5.00x stays at 5.00x (respects limit)","-- ✅ Smooth animation with cubicOut easing","-- ✅ No console errors","-","-### F121: Cross-platform scroll/input normalization - COMPLETE","-","-**Objective:**","-Normalize scroll sensitivity across macOS/Windows/Linux and trackpad/mouse.","-","-**Implementation:**","-","-1. **deltaMode Normalization:**","-   - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px)","-   - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px)","-   - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly","-","-2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:**","-   - Track event frequency with timestamps","-   - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior","-   - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad","-   - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode","-","-3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:**","-   - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003","-   - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events)","-","-4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:**","-   - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5","-   - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events","-","-**Constants Added:**","-```typescript","-LINE_HEIGHT = 40                    // Pixels per line","-PAGE_HEIGHT = 800                   // Pixels per page","-MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003      // Base zoom sensitivity","-TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001   // Lower for trackpad","-MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5      // Cap per event","-TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20   // ms between events","-TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3  // Rapid events to confirm","-```","-","-**Files Changed:**","-- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (normalizeWheelDelta function, updated handleWheel)","-- `features.json` (F120, F121 passes: true)","-","-**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**","-- ✅ Scroll down (deltaY=300) zooms out: 1.00x → 0.61x","-- ✅ Scroll up (deltaY=-500) zooms in: 0.61x → 1.00x","-- ✅ No console errors","-- ✅ Smooth zoom animation","-","-**Next:** F116 (Performance), F117 (Visual parity)","-","----","-","-## Session: 2025-12-16 (F118 - Momentum Panning) - COMPLETE ✅","-","-### F118: Momentum/Inertia Panning - COMPLETE","-","-**Objective:**","-Implement velocity-based drag with momentum/inertia like Google Maps and iOS.","-","-**Implementation:**","-","-1. **Velocity Tracking:**","-   - Track pointer position samples during drag with timestamps","-   - Store last N samples (5) for velocity calculation","-   - Calculate total displacement and time to get velocity","-","-2. **Momentum Animation:**","-   - On pointer release, calculate average velocity from samples","-   - Start `requestAnimationFrame` loop with initial velocity","-   - Apply friction (0.93) per frame to gradually slow down","-   - Stop when velocity < 0.5 world units","-","-3. **Integration:**","-   - Momentum stops on any user interaction (new drag, zoom)","-   - Cleanup animation on component unmount","-   - COMPLETE ✅\n\n### @zazcart/tools Critical Performance Fixes - ALL COMPLETE\n\n**Objective:**\nComplete audit and fix all performance issues in @zazcart/tools state machine library identified in FIXME files.\n\n**Fixes Applied (7 total):**\n\n1. **Unsafe Listener Unsubscription** (store.ts)\n   - Copy listeners array before iteration to prevent issues during unsubscribe\n   - Prevents ConcurrentModificationException-like bugs\n\n2. **Validation on Hot Path** (context.ts)\n   - Skip schema validation in production for performance\n   - Only validate in development (NODE_ENV !== 'production')\n\n3. **devLog Crash Risk** (logs.ts)\n   - Safe error handling instead of throwing in devLog\n\n4. **O(N²) reduceObject** (object-utils.ts)\n   - Changed from spread operator in loop to Object.assign\n   - O(N) implementation now\n\n5. **GC Thrashing - Metadata Option** (machine.ts, models/machine.ts)\n   - Added `metadata?: boolean` option to MachineConfig\n   - When `false`, skips metadata updates on dispatch\n   - Prevents GC thrashing from object allocations at 60fps\n\n6. **V8 De-optimization** (hooks.ts)\n   - Clean object return instead of hybrid array+object\n   - Breaking change: `useMachine()` returns `{ state, actions, dispatch }`\n\n7. **Double Proxying** (machine.ts)\n   - Direct assignment instead of Immer produce() for selectors\n   - Reduces proxy overhead in hot path\n\n**FIXME Files Deleted:**\n- packages/toolkit/FIXME_STATE_MACHINE.md\n- packages/toolkit/FIXME_HELPERS.md\n- packages/toolkit/FIXME_SCHEMA.md\n- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/FIXME.md\n\n**Canvas Integration:**\n- Applied `metadata: false` to canvas-machine.ts for 60fps optimization\n\n**TypeScript Fixes:**\n- Fixed reduceObject return type in object-utils.ts\n- Fixed check() return type in context.ts\n\n**Tests:**\n- 216 pass, 4 fail (pre-existing stack trace test failures in error-utils)\n\n**QA Visual Verification (Playwright MCP):**\n- ✅ App running at 120fps (green FPS counter visible)\n- ✅ All LOD levels rendering correctly (MACRO/MESO/MICRO)\n- ✅ Sidebar with 50 trains visible\n- ✅ Minimap functional\n- ✅ Timeline header with dates\n- ✅ No console errors\n\n**Files Modified:**\n- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/store.ts\n- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/context.ts\n- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/machine.ts\n- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/models/machine.ts\n- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/hooks.ts\n- packages/toolkit/src/helpers/logs.ts\n- packages/toolkit/src/helpers/object-utils.ts\n- apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts\n- apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts\n- apps/canvas/src/state/interaction.ts\n\n**Impact:**\n- Eliminated GC thrashing for high-frequency state machines\n- V8 hidden class optimization for better JIT performance\n- O(N) instead of O(N²) for object reduction\n- Production-only validation for performance\n- All features still passing (53 complete)\n\n**Remaining TODOs (Documented Deferrals):**\n- Momentum scrolling reimplementation (deferred for STATE-ARCH-001 Phase 2)\n- PERF-002 Phase 2 actual culling (infrastructure in place)\n\n---\n\n## Session: 2025-12-23 (HARNESS-006 + CLI Update) -
